contributions.statement: The AI Scientist — an end-to-end, fully automated pipeline in which a frontier LLM generates research ideas, searches the literature, designs and executes experiments, visualizes results, writes a complete scientific manuscript, and performs self-review in an open-ended loop — enables autonomous scientific discovery in machine learning at a cost of under $15 per paper.
contributions.quote: We introduce the first end-to-end framework for fully automated scientific discovery in Machine Learning research, enabled by frontier LLMs (Section 3). This fully automated process includes idea generation, experiment design, execution, and visualizing and writing up the results into a full manuscript.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce the first end-to-end framework for fully automated scientific discovery in Machine
Learning research, enabled by frontier LLMs (Section 3). This fully automated process includes
idea generation, experiment design, execution, and visualizing and writing up the results into a
full manuscript.

contributions.statement: An automated, foundation-model-based paper-reviewing process achieves near-human-level performance (65% vs. 66% balanced accuracy) when evaluated against ICLR 2022 OpenReview data, enabling scalable quality assessment of LLM-generated research papers.
contributions.quote: To assess the quality of the generated papers, we introduce a foundation model-based reviewing process in Section 4. This process achieves near-human-level performance across multiple evaluation metrics (e.g. 65% vs. 66% balanced accuracy) when evaluated on ICLR 2022 OpenReview data.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
To assess the quality of the generated papers, we introduce a foundation model-based reviewing
process in Section 4. This process achieves near-human-level performance across multiple evalu-
ation metrics (e.g. 65% vs. 66% balanced accuracy) when evaluated on ICLR 2022 OpenReview
dat

contributions.statement: Fully automated LLM-driven scientific discovery can produce novel machine learning papers in diffusion modeling, transformer-based language modeling, and learning dynamics that exceed the acceptance threshold of a top machine learning conference as judged by an automated reviewer.
contributions.quote: The AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by our automated reviewer... we focus on a subset of these papers, highlighting novel insights in diffusion modeling, language modeling, and grokking.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
. The AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the
acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by our automated reviewer. This
approach signifies the beginning of a new era in scientific discovery in machine learning: bringing
the transformati

contributions.statement: The AI Scientist-v2 — an end-to-end agentic system for automated scientific discovery that eliminates reliance on human-authored code templates, uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by a dedicated experiment manager agent, and integrates VLM feedback for iterative figure refinement — can autonomously formulate hypotheses, design and execute experiments, analyze data, and author complete scientific manuscripts across diverse machine learning domains.
contributions.quote: We introduce The AI Scientist-v2, an automated scientific discovery framework enhanced by agentic tree search, VLM feedback, and parallel experiment execution. It thereby significantly improves the autonomy, flexibility, and scientific exploration depth of previous systems.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce The AI Scientist-v2, an automated scientific discovery framework enhanced
by agentic tree search, VLM feedback, and parallel experiment execution. It thereby significantly
improves the autonomy, flexibility, and scientific exploration depth of previous systems.

contributions.statement: A fully AI-generated manuscript can successfully pass peer review at a recognized machine learning workshop, with one AI-generated submission exceeding the average human acceptance threshold at an ICLR workshop.
contributions.quote: We demonstrate, for the first time, that an AI-generated manuscript can successfully pass peer review at a recognized machine learning workshop, marking a critical milestone for AI science.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We demonstrate, for the first time, that an AI-generated manuscript can successfully pass peer
review at a recognized machine learning workshop, marking a critical milestone for AI science.

contributions.statement: Comprehensive internal evaluation and analysis of peer-review feedback on AI-generated manuscripts reveals the strengths, weaknesses, and current status of fully autonomous scientific writing relative to human-authored publications.
contributions.quote: We conduct comprehensive internal evaluations and analyses of both peer-review feedback and our system's outputs, providing insights into the strengths, weaknesses, and current status of AI-generated manuscripts relative to traditional human-authored scientific publications.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We conduct comprehensive internal evaluations and analyses of both peer-review feedback and
our system’s outputs, providing insights into the strengths, weaknesses, and current status of
AI-generated manuscripts relative to traditional human-authored scientific publications.

contributions.statement: An open-source codebase and ICLR 2025 workshop experiment data for The AI Scientist-v2 are released to enable further research into automated scientific discovery.
contributions.quote: We open-source the full codebase for The AI Scientist-v2 and the ICLR 2025 workshop experiment data, encouraging further exploration by the research community and advancing a discussion regarding AI's evolving role in science–in the open.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We open-source the full codebase for The AI Scientist-v2 and the ICLR 2025 workshop
experiment data, encouraging further exploration by the research community and advancing a
discussion regarding AI’s evolving role in science–in the open.

contributions.statement: Jr. AI Scientist — an autonomous AI scientist system that, given a baseline paper, its LaTeX source files, and associated codebase from a human mentor, identifies the paper's limitations, formulates improvement hypotheses, iteratively runs experiments on complex multi-file codebases using modern coding agents (e.g., Claude Code), and writes a full research paper from the results.
contributions.quote: we introduce Jr. AI Scientist, a new AI Scientist that mimics the essential research workflow of a novice student researcher: Starting from a baseline paper, it identifies key limitations, proposes an improvement hypothesis, iteratively conduct experiments until improvements are achieved, and writes a paper with the results.
contributions.quote_verbatim: paper, we introduce Jr. AI Scientist, a new AI Scientist that mimics the essential research workflow of a novice student researcher: Starting from a baseline paper, it identifies key limitations, proposes an improvement hypothesis, validates it through rigorous experimentation, and writes a paper with the results. Table ˜ 1

contributions.statement: A 'Jr. AI Scientist' problem setting that reframes fully autonomous science into a tractable, mentor-guided baseline-extension task — giving the AI agent one baseline paper and its codebase as the starting point — providing a clear optimization direction and enabling scientifically valuable, complex-codebase contributions.
contributions.quote: A Jr. AI Scientist is defined as an AI Scientist that is given baseline resources and focuses on extending the baseline. This setting is novice student analog... This problem setting reframes previously ambitious goals into a more specific objective, providing a clear optimization direction for the AI Scientist.
contributions.quote_verbatim: validates it through rigorous experimentation, and writes a paper with the results. Table ˜ 1 shows a comparison of the problem setting with existing research. This problem setting reframes previously ambitious goals into a more specific objective, providing a clear optimization direction for the AI Scientist. M

contributions.statement: Autonomous AI scientist systems that start from a single baseline paper and operate on real multi-file codebases achieve substantially higher automated peer-review scores (DeepReviewer) than prior fully automated systems that assume no baseline or are restricted to single-file code.
contributions.quote: the papers generated by Jr. AI Scientist achieved substantially higher review scores compared to the existing AI-generated papers. Therefore, our Jr. AI Scientist can be regarded as the most capable autonomous AI Scientist.
contributions.quote_verbatim: the papers generated by Jr. AI Scientist achieved substantially higher review scores compared to the existing AI-generated papers. Therefore, our Jr. AI Scientist can be regarded as the most capable autonomous AI Scientist.

contributions.statement: A comprehensive risk taxonomy for autonomous AI scientist systems, cataloguing concrete failure modes and hazards across the idea-generation, experimentation, writing, and review phases, derived from end-to-end system development and deployment experience.
contributions.quote: we comprehensively report various risks identified during development. We believe this study clarifies the current role and limitations of AI Scientist systems, offering insights into the areas that still require human expertise and the risks that may emerge as these systems evolve.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we comprehensively report various risks identified during development. We believe this study clarifies the current role and limitations of AI Scientist systems, offering insights into the areas that still require human expertise and the risks that may emerge as these systems evolve.

contributions.statement: ResearchAgent — an LLM-powered system that automatically identifies novel research problems, proposes methods, and designs experiments by augmenting a core paper with an academic-graph neighbourhood and an entity-centric knowledge store, then iteratively refines the output via feedback from collaborative ReviewingAgents.
contributions.quote: we propose ResearchAgent, which leverages the encyclopedic knowledge and linguistic reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to assist them in their work. This system automatically defines novel problems, proposes methods and designs experiments, while iteratively refining them based on the feedback from collaborative LLM-powered reviewing agents.

contributions.statement: An entity-centric knowledge store built from co-occurrences of key concepts across scientific literature enables cross-domain idea generation by capturing novel underlying relationships within and across fields.
contributions.quote: to develop an encyclopedic view of related concepts, we build and then augment ResearchAgent with an entity-centric knowledge store derived from co-occurrences of key concepts in the scientific literature. This repository is aimed at capturing novel underlying relationships within and across domains, thereby increasing the chances of a cross-pollination of ideas

contributions.statement: LLM-based ReviewingAgents prompted with evaluation criteria induced from real researchers' judgements provide iterative, human-preference-aligned feedback that measurably improves research idea quality.
contributions.quote: to simulate robust feedback mechanisms, we instantiate a number of LLM-powered ReviewingAgents that help the ResearchAgent to iterate on research idea generation with constructive critiques. Crucially, these ReviewingAgents are prompted with evaluation criteria that are induced from real researchers' judgements, thus aligning them with actual scientific preferential standards.

contributions.statement: Combining an entity-centric knowledge store with iterative LLM-based peer review produces research ideas that are significantly more novel, clear, and relevant than those generated by strong LLM baselines, as validated by both human and model-based evaluation across multiple scientific disciplines.
contributions.quote: on a battery of tests conducted with both human- and model-based evaluations, we demonstrate that ResearchAgent outperforms strong LLM-powered baselines by large margins, generating more clear, relevant, and significant ideas that are especially novel. Furthermore, analyses show the efficacy of our comprehensive approach to modeling ResearchAgent: the entity-centric knowledge store and the iterative idea refinement steps help the system generate meaningfully better ideas

contributions.statement: HypoRefine — a method that grounds data-driven hypothesis generation (via HypoGeniC) with literature-based insights through a collaborative agent architecture that refines and maintains a shared hypothesis pool — outperforms literature-only, data-only, and few-shot baselines on out-of-distribution generalization across five social-science classification tasks (15.75%, 3.37%, and 8.97% gains, respectively).
contributions.quote: We propose the first approach to using both literature information and data for LLM-powered hypothesis generation.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We propose the first approach to using both literature information and data for LLM-powered hypothesis generation.

contributions.statement: Automatic evaluation across five datasets shows that integrating literature-based and data-driven hypothesis generation yields the best generalizability, measured by out-of-distribution accuracy, across all task and model configurations, and that the generated hypotheses transfer effectively to different inference models.
contributions.quote: Experiments on five datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Hypotheses generated by combining information from literature and data achieves the best performance across all task and model configurations.
contributions.quote_verbatim: mplementation and Baselines 4 Results 4.1 Automatic Evaluation Hypotheses generated by combining information from literature and data achieves the best performance across all task and model configurations ( Table 1 ).

contributions.statement: LLM-generated hypotheses improve human decision-making accuracy: providing them as guidance raises human accuracy by 7.44% on deception detection and 14.19% on AI-generated content detection, establishing a human-evaluation protocol for assessing the utility of machine-generated hypotheses.
contributions.quote: We conduct the first human evaluation to test the utility of LLM-generated hypotheses and demonstrate consistent improvements on two challenging tasks.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We conduct the first human evaluation to test the utility of LLM-generated hypotheses and demonstrate consistent improvements on two challenging tasks.

contributions.statement: Literature-based and data-driven hypotheses are empirically complementary: each set systematically contains novel information absent from the other, supporting their combination rather than substitution.
contributions.quote: we find that literature-based and data-driven hypotheses complement each other, as one set often contains novel information not found in the other set.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we find that literature-based and data-driven hypotheses complement each other, as one set often contains novel information not found in the other set.

contributions.statement: NovBench — a large-scale benchmark of 1,684 paper–review pairs from a leading NLP conference, pairing novelty descriptions extracted from paper introductions with expert-written novelty evaluations — enables systematic evaluation of LLMs on the task of academic novelty assessment.
contributions.quote: We introducing a structured benchmark for novelty evaluation. This resource, which incorporates the textual evaluations of the novelty dimension of the reviewers alongside the novelty descriptions of the paper introductions, constitutes a critical resource for future research.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introducing a structured benchmark for novelty evaluation. This resource, which incorporates the’ textual evaluations of the novelty dimension of the reviewers alongside the novelty descriptions of the paper introductions, constitutes a critical resource for future research

contributions.statement: A four-dimensional evaluation framework (Relevance, Correctness, Coverage, and Clarity) assesses the quality of LLM-generated novelty review text in a semantics-aware, interpretable manner that surpasses lexical overlap metrics such as ROUGE and BLEU.
contributions.quote: We propose a four-dimensional, interpretable, and semantics-aware framework for evaluating LLM-generated novelty review text. This metric suite surpasses the limitations of traditional lexical overlap metrics (e.g., ROUGE/BLEU) and more effectively captures the quality of "novelty" evaluation in free-form text.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We propose a four-dimensional, interpretable, and semantics-aware framework for evaluating LLM-generated novelty review text. This metric suite surpasses the limitations of traditional lexical overlap metrics (e.g., ROUGE/BLEU) and more effectively captures the quality of "novelty" evaluation in free-form text.

contributions.statement: Systematic benchmarking of general-purpose and specialized LLMs on novelty evaluation reveals that current models exhibit limited understanding of scientific novelty and that fine-tuned models frequently suffer from instruction-following deficiencies, exposing a substantial gap between LLM-generated and human novelty evaluations.
contributions.quote: We conduct systematic evaluation and benchmarking of current general LLMs (e.g., GPT-5, Gemini-2.5-flash) and specialized LLMs on the novelty evaluation task. Based on this benchmark, we further analyze the gap between LLM-generated evaluations and human judgments, providing an in-depth understanding of these models' advantages and limitations in identifying and articulating novelty.
contributions.quote_verbatim: valuation and benchmarking of current general LLMs (e.g., GPT-5 (OpenAI, 2025 ) , Gemini-2.5-flash (Gemini Team, 2025 ) ) and specialized LLMs on the novelty evaluation task. Based on this benchmark, we further analyze the gap between LLM-generated evaluations and human judgments, providing an in-depth understanding of these models’ advantages and limitations in identifying and artic

contributions.statement: Comprehensive empirical analysis of prompting strategies and model types identifies key factors for generating high-quality novelty review text and characterizes behavioral patterns of LLMs in novelty evaluation, pointing toward targeted fine-tuning strategies that jointly improve novelty comprehension and instruction adherence.
contributions.quote: We conduct a comprehensive empirical analysis that identifies key factors for generating high-quality, highly interpretable novelty review text, and reveals important behavioral patterns of LLMs in novelty evaluation, thus directing the future development of more reliable AI-assisted peer review.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We conduct a comprehensive empirical analysis that identifies key factors for generating high-quality, highly interpretable novelty review text, and reveals important behavioral patterns of LLMs in novelty evaluation, thus directing the future development of more reliable AI-assisted peer review.

contributions.statement: PaperQA — a modular, agent-based retrieval-augmented generation system that decomposes RAG into discrete tools (paper search, evidence gathering, answer synthesis) and lets an LLM orchestrate them dynamically, enabling iterative, multi-hop question answering over full-text scientific articles with cited provenance.
contributions.quote: We present PaperQA, a RAG agent for answering questions over the scientific literature. PaperQA is an agent that performs information retrieval across full-text scientific articles, assesses the relevance of sources and passages, and uses RAG to provide answers.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We present PaperQA, a RAG agent for answering questions over the scientific literature. PaperQA is an agent that performs information retrieval across full-text scientific articles, assesses the relevance of sources and passages, and uses RAG to provide answers.

contributions.statement: LitQA — a benchmark of questions that require retrieval and synthesis of information from full-text scientific papers (not just abstracts) and cover literature published after common LLM training cutoffs, enabling evaluation of open-retrieval scientific reasoning beyond existing abstract-based QA datasets.
contributions.quote: we also introduce LitQA, a more complex benchmark that requires retrieval and synthesis of information from full-text scientific papers across the literature
contributions.quote_verbatim: we also introduce LitQA, a more complex benchmark that requires retrieval and synthesis of information from full-text scientific papers across the literature

contributions.statement: An agent-based RAG system for scientific literature surpasses existing LLMs and LLM agents on standard science QA benchmarks, including outperforming GPT-4 by 30 percentage points on closed-book PubMedQA (57.9 % → 86.3 %).
contributions.quote: we find it exceeds performance of existing LLMs and LLM agents on current science QA benchmarks … we found PaperQA beats GPT-4 by 30 points (57.9% to 86.3%)
contributions.quote_verbatim: we find it exceeds performance of existing LLMs and LLM agents on current science QA benchmarks. To push the field closer to how humans perform research on

contributions.statement: A retrieval-augmented agent for scientific QA can match expert human researchers in both accuracy and time-on-task on a full-text retrieval benchmark while being substantially cheaper than human expert labor.
contributions.quote: we demonstrate PaperQA's matches expert human researchers on LitQA … PaperQA outperforms all models tested and commercial tools, and is comparable to human experts on LitQA on performance and time, but is significantly cheaper in terms of costs
contributions.quote_verbatim: ieve information outside of the underlying LLM’s pre-training data. PaperQA outperforms all models tested and commercial tools, and is comparable to human experts on LitQA on performance and time, but is significantly cheaper in terms of costs.

contributions.statement: A dataset of 79,302 adversarial, aspect-guided text edits spanning nine perturbation aspects (contribution, soundness, presentation, tone, factual correctness, conclusion, and rebuttal-level variants) applied across papers, reviews, and rebuttals in the academic peer review setting.
contributions.quote: We curate a dataset comprising 79,302 adversarial, aspect-guided text edits, which incorporate perturbations across nine distinct aspects affecting reviews, papers, and rebuttals, all guided by our perturbation aspect taxonomy.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We curate a dataset comprising 79,302 adversarial, aspect-guided text edits, which incorporate perturbations across nine distinct aspects affecting reviews, papers, and rebuttals, all guided by our perturbation aspect taxonomy.

contributions.statement: An aspect-guided, multi-level perturbation framework for diagnosing LLM robustness in automated peer review, targeting single components (paper, review, or rebuttal) along defined quality aspects and assessing impact through directional and invariance statistical tests on both LLM-as-Reviewer and LLM-as-Meta-Reviewer roles.
contributions.quote: Our aspect-guided, multi-level approach effectively integrates both LLM reviewer and meta-reviewer perturbations, employing directional and invariance tests to assess the impact of these perturbations.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Our aspect-guided, multi-level approach effectively integrates both LLM reviewer and meta-reviewer perturbations, employing directional and invariance tests to assess the impact of these perturbations.

contributions.statement: LLM-based peer review pipelines exhibit systematic vulnerabilities: 'strong reject' review conclusions disproportionately dominate meta-review decisions, hostile or erroneous critiques are misinterpreted as thoroughness, and incomplete or hostile rebuttals can unexpectedly raise acceptance rates; these biases persist across multiple Chain-of-Thought prompting strategies.
contributions.quote: Our findings illuminate vulnerabilities within the LLM-based review system, revealing that the 'strong reject' conclusion can overshadow all other feedback. Furthermore, hostile or erroneous critiques may be misinterpreted as thoroughness… Statistical tests show that these biases persist under various Chain-of-Thought prompting strategies.
contributions.quote_verbatim: s. • Our findings illuminate vulnerabilities within the LLM-based review system, revealing that the “strong reject” conclusion can overshadow all other feedback. Furthermore, hostile or erroneous critiques may be misinterpreted as thoroughness, underscoring the urgent need for enhanced safeguards. 2 Perturbation Design We present our frame

contributions.statement: Denario — a modular AI multi-agent system with specialized modules for idea generation, literature review, research planning, code writing and execution, plotting, and paper drafting and review — enables end-to-end autonomous scientific research across diverse disciplines.
contributions.quote: We present Denario, an AI multi-agent system designed to serve as a scientific research assistant. Denario can perform many different tasks, such as generating ideas, checking the literature, developing research plans, writing and executing code, making plots, and drafting and reviewing a scientific paper.
contributions.quote_verbatim: e present Denario , an AI multi-agent system designed to serve as a scientific research assistant. Denario can perform many different tasks, such as generating ideas, checking the literature, developing research plans, writing and executing code, making plots, and drafting and reviewing a scientific paper.

contributions.statement: A corpus of AI-generated scientific papers produced by Denario across thirteen domains — including astrophysics, biology, biophysics, chemistry, materials science, mathematical physics, medicine, neuroscience, and planetary science — together with domain-expert evaluations providing numerical scores and review-like feedback, benchmarks the current capabilities and limitations of autonomous AI research systems.
contributions.quote: we illustrate its capabilities by presenting multiple AI-generated papers generated by it in many different scientific disciplines such as astrophysics, biology, biophysics, biomedical informatics, chemistry, material science, mathematical physics, medicine, neuroscience and planetary science. We report the evaluations performed on these papers by domain experts, who provided both numerical scores and review-like feedback.
contributions.quote_verbatim: illustrate its capabilities by presenting multiple AI-generated papers generated by it in many different scientific disciplines such as astrophysics, biology, biophysics, biomedical informatics, chemistry, material science, mathematical physics, medicine, neuroscience and planetary science. Denario also excels at combining ideas from different disciplines, and we illustrate this by showing a paper that applies methods fro

contributions.statement: Cross-disciplinary AI-driven research that combines methods from quantum physics and machine learning to analyze astrophysical data is achievable within a single automated multi-agent pipeline.
contributions.quote: Denario also excels at combining ideas from different disciplines, and we illustrate this by showing a paper that applies methods from quantum physics and machine learning to astrophysical data.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Denario also excels at combining ideas from different disciplines, and we illustrate this by showing a paper that applies methods from quantum physics and machine learning to astrophysical data.

contributions.statement: Agent Laboratory — an autonomous LLM-based pipeline that accepts a human-provided research idea and completes the full research cycle (literature review, experimentation, and report writing) to produce a code repository and research report, with optional human feedback at each stage.
contributions.quote: we introduce Agent Laboratory, an autonomous LLM-based framework capable of completing the entire research process. This framework accepts a human-provided research idea and progresses through three stages–literature review, experimentation, and report writing–in order to produce research, including a code repository and a research report, while enabling users to provide feedback and guidance at each stage.

contributions.statement: Autonomous research assistance that reduces research expenses by 84% compared to prior autonomous research methods while enabling generated ML code to achieve state-of-the-art performance.
contributions.quote: Agent Laboratory reduces research expenses, achieving an 84% decrease compared to previous autonomous research methods.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
gent Laboratory reduces research expenses,
achieving an 84% decrease compared to previ-
ous autonomous research methods

contributions.statement: A co-pilot mode for LLM-driven research pipelines, in which human reviewers approve or request revision of agent outputs at the end of each subtask phase, improves overall research quality over fully autonomous operation.
contributions.quote: Incorporating human involvement improves the overall quality of research... In co-pilot mode, besides providing the initial idea, a human reviews the output at the end of each subtask phase (e.g., literature review, generated report). The human reviewer can then approve progression to the next subtask or request the agent repeat the current subtask, providing high-level notes for improvement.

contributions.statement: PaperQA2, a retrieval-augmented generation agent with agentic tool use (paper search, evidence gathering with LLM reranking and contextual summarization, citation traversal, and answer generation), matches or exceeds PhD-level subject matter expert performance on scientific literature retrieval and summarization tasks without restricting humans in time, tools, or internet access.
contributions.quote: We show that PaperQA2, a frontier language model agent optimized for improved factuality, matches or exceeds subject matter expert performance on three realistic literature research tasks without any restrictions on humans (i.e., full access to internet, search tools, and time).
contributions.quote_verbatim: We show that PaperQA2, a frontier language model agent optimized for improved factuality, matches or exceeds subject matter expert performance on three realistic literature research tasks without any restrictions on humans (i.e., full access to internet, search tools, and time).

contributions.statement: LitQA2, a benchmark of 248 multiple-choice questions requiring full-literature retrieval to answer, where answers appear only in paper bodies (not abstracts) and ideally only once in all scientific literature, provides a rigorous evaluation of scientific question-answering that includes a direct human performance comparison.
contributions.quote: We also introduce a hard benchmark for scientific literature research called LitQA2 that guided design of PaperQA2, leading to it exceeding human performance.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We also introduce a hard benchmark for scientific literature research called LitQA2 that guided design of PaperQA2, leading to it exceeding human performance.

contributions.statement: A rigorous human-AI comparison methodology for evaluating language model agents on real-world scientific literature tasks — information retrieval, summarization, and contradiction detection — with unrestricted human baselines (full internet, tools, and time) enables meaningful assessment of whether AI systems are suitable for scientific research.
contributions.quote: We developed a rigorous human-AI comparison methodology to evaluate language model agents on real-world literature search tasks covering information retrieval, summarization, and contradiction detection tasks.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We developed a rigorous human-AI comparison methodology to evaluate language model agents on real-world literature search tasks covering information retrieval, summarization, and contradiction detection tasks.

contributions.statement: Automated contradiction detection in the scientific literature, applied at scale, identifies a mean of 2.34 contradictions per biology paper, with 70% of detected contradictions validated by human experts, demonstrating that AI agents can surface inconsistencies across the literature faster and at greater scale than human researchers.
contributions.quote: Finally, we apply PaperQA2 to identify contradictions within the scientific literature, an important scientific task that is challenging for humans. PaperQA2 identifies 2.34 +/- 1.99 contradictions per paper in a random subset of biology papers, of which 70% are validated by human experts.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Finally, we apply PaperQA2 to identify contradictions within the scientific literature, an important scientific task that is challenging for humans. PaperQA2 identifies (mean SD, papers) contradictions per paper in a random subset of biology papers, of which are validated by human experts.

contributions.statement: Cited, Wikipedia-style scientific topic summaries generated by a language agent are significantly more accurate than existing human-written Wikipedia articles on the same topics.
contributions.quote: PaperQA2 writes cited, Wikipedia-style summaries of scientific topics that are significantly more accurate than existing, human-written Wikipedia articles.
contributions.quote_verbatim: PaperQA2 writes cited, Wikipedia-style summaries of scientific topics that are significantly more accurate than existing, human-written Wikipedia articles.

contributions.statement: A five-field audit schema for scoring the disclosure completeness of LLM agent benchmark evaluation runs, covering benchmark identity, harness specification, inference settings, cost reporting, and failure breakdown, together with a single aggregate score, designed to be answerable from a paper and its public repository in roughly one hour without running any model.
contributions.quote: We designed a small audit schema (five fields: benchmark identity, harness specification, inference settings, cost reporting, failure breakdown), wrote a scoring codebook with the boundary cases we hit during pilot scoring, applied it to twelve canonical benchmark papers (eight agent, four classical static), and recorded what we saw.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We designed a small audit schema (five fields: benchmark identity, harness specification, inference settings, cost reporting, failure breakdown), wrote a scoring codebook with the boundary cases we hit during pilot scoring, applied it to twelve canonical benchmark papers (eight agent, four classical static), and recorded what we saw.

contributions.statement: A scoring codebook that documents the partial-versus-disclosed boundary rules for each field in the agent-run audit schema, derived from boundary cases encountered during pilot scoring that were not obvious in advance.
contributions.quote: Second, a codebook that documents the partial-versus-disclosed boundary rules for each field. Several of those rules were not obvious in advance and only emerged after we hit cases we disagreed with ourselves on.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Second, a codebook that documents the partial-versus-disclosed boundary rules for each field. Several of those rules were not obvious in advance and only emerged after we hit cases we disagreed with ourselves on.

contributions.statement: Empirical finding: across eight agent benchmark papers, mean disclosure score is 0.38 out of 1.0, versus 0.66 for four classical static benchmark papers; none of the eight agent benchmark papers disclose inference cost in any form, and none fully disclose a content-addressed container image of the evaluation environment.
contributions.quote: The mean audit score across the eight agent-benchmark papers is 0.38 (out of 1.0), and across the four classical static benchmarks 0.66; the largest gap is on cost (none of the eight agent benchmark papers disclose inference cost in any form) and on harness specification (none fully disclose a content-addressed container image of the evaluation environment).
contributions.quote_verbatim: e mean audit score across the eight agent-benchmark papers is 0.38 (out of 1.0), and across the four classical static benchmarks 0.66; the largest gap is on cost (none of the eight agent benchmark papers disclose inference cost in any form) and on harness specification (none of the eight fully disclose a content-addressed container image of the evaluation en

contributions.statement: A released set of open artifacts for auditing LLM agent benchmark disclosure: a JSON Schema file defining the audit schema, a Markdown codebook, and a CSV scoring sheet covering twelve canonical benchmark papers.
contributions.quote: We release the schema as a JSON Schema file, the codebook as a Markdown document, and the raw scoring sheet as a CSV.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We release the schema as a JSON Schema file, the codebook as a Markdown document, and the raw scoring sheet as a CSV.

contributions.statement: MemoNoveltyAgent — a multi-agent workflow that combines citation-network-based retrieval, hierarchical abstract memory of research evolutionary trajectories, point-wise novelty decomposition, and self-validation to generate comprehensive and faithful paper novelty assessment reports.
contributions.quote: We propose MemoNoveltyAgent, a multi-agent workflow for comprehensive and faithful paper novelty report generation.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We propose MemoNoveltyAgent, a multi-agent workflow for comprehensive and faithful paper novelty report generation.

contributions.statement: A historical research memory built from large-scale scholarly corpora that organizes research into hierarchical trees to distill field-specific evolutionary trajectories, combined with citation-network-based retrieval, point-wise decomposition, and self-validation, improves faithfulness and depth of automated novelty reports.
contributions.quote: We build a historical research memory and combine it with citation-network-based retrieval, point-wise decomposition, and self-validation to improve report faithfulness.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We build a historical research memory and combine it with citation-network-based retrieval, point-wise decomposition, and self-validation to improve report faithfulness.

contributions.statement: A RAG-integrated checklist evaluation method with dense question coverage enables reliable, evidence-grounded assessment of open-ended novelty report generation, validated through human alignment studies.
contributions.quote: We introduce a RAG-integrated checklist evaluation method and validate its effectiveness and reliability through human alignment.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introduce a RAG-integrated checklist evaluation method and validate its effectiveness and reliability through human alignment.

contributions.statement: A controlled experimental framework for comparing human and LLM research idea generation — enforcing style standardization, matched topic distributions, and blind expert review — enables statistically rigorous head-to-head evaluation of ideation capabilities for the first time.
contributions.quote: We address this by establishing an experimental design that evaluates research idea generation while controlling for confounders and performs the first head-to-head comparison between expert NLP researchers and an LLM ideation agent.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We address this by establishing an experimental design that evaluates research idea generation while controlling for confounders and performs the first head-to-head comparison between expert NLP researchers and an LLM ideation agent.

contributions.statement: In a large-scale blind evaluation by over 100 NLP researchers, LLM-generated research ideas are rated as significantly more novel (p < 0.05) than ideas written by human experts, while being judged slightly weaker on feasibility — the first statistically significant conclusion on LLM research ideation capability.
contributions.quote: we obtain the first statistically significant conclusion on current LLM capabilities for research ideation: we find LLM-generated ideas are judged as more novel (p < 0.05) than human expert ideas while being judged slightly weaker on feasibility
contributions.quote_verbatim: as, we obtain the first statistically significant conclusion on current LLM capabilities for research ideation: we find LLM-generated ideas are judged as more novel ( ) than human expert ideas while being judged slightly weaker on feasibility. S

contributions.statement: LLM ideation agents exhibit two systematic failure modes at scale: lack of diversity in generated ideas and unreliable self-evaluation of idea quality.
contributions.quote: we identify open problems in building and evaluating research agents, including failures of LLM self-evaluation and their lack of diversity in generation
contributions.quote_verbatim: we identify open problems in building and evaluating research agents, including failures of LLM self-evaluation and their lack of diversity in generation

contributions.statement: An LLM ideation agent combining retrieval-augmented generation with an overgenerate-and-rerank inference-time scaling strategy produces research ideas rated as more novel than those written by human NLP experts in blind review.
contributions.quote: We compare our human expert baseline with a simple and effective LLM agent that incorporates retrieval augmentation and adopts recent ideas in inference-time scaling, such as overgenerating and reranking LM outputs.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We compare our human expert baseline with a simple and effective LLM agent that incorporates retrieval augmentation and adopts recent ideas in inference-time scaling, such as overgenerating and reranking LM outputs.

contributions.statement: An end-to-end study design that recruits expert researchers to execute generated ideas into full research projects enables ground-truth measurement of whether expert novelty and feasibility judgments translate into meaningful differences in research outcomes.
contributions.quote: we propose an end-to-end study design which recruits researchers to execute these ideas into full projects, enabling us to study whether these novelty and feasibility judgements result in meaningful differences in research outcome
contributions.quote_verbatim: d propose an end-to-end study design which recruits researchers to execute these ideas into full projects, enabling us to study whether these novelty and feasibility judgements result in meaningful differences in research outcome.

contributions.statement: A hybrid framework (LLMCG) combining LLM-based causal relation extraction from large psychology literature corpora with link prediction on the resulting causal graph generates novel psychological hypotheses that match expert (doctoral scholar) quality in novelty and outperform LLM-only hypothesis generation.
contributions.quote: our study introduces a groundbreaking approach for computational hypothesis generation in psychology. We analyzed 43,312 psychology articles using a LLM to extract causal relation pairs. This analysis produced a specialized causal graph for psychology. Applying link prediction algorithms, we generated 130 potential psychological hypotheses focusing on "well-being", then compared them against research ideas conceived by doctoral scholars and those produced solely by the LLM. Interestingly, our combined approach of a LLM and causal graphs mirrored the expert-level insights in terms of novelty, clearly surpassing the LLM-only hypotheses
contributions.quote_verbatim:

contributions.statement: A domain-specific causal knowledge graph for psychology, constructed by extracting causal relation pairs from 43,312 peer-reviewed psychology articles using GPT-4, serves as a structured reservoir of causal knowledge for downstream hypothesis generation and causal analysis.
contributions.quote: it extracts invaluable causal knowledge from the literature and converts it to visual graphics. These aids can feed algorithms to help deduce more latent causal relations and guide models in generating a plethora of novel causal hypotheses

contributions.statement: LLM-plus-causal-graph hybrid hypothesis generation produces semantically richer and more conceptually diverse hypotheses than LLM-only generation, as demonstrated by deep semantic analysis showing broader conceptual incorporation and wider semantic spectrum.
contributions.quote: through deep semantic analysis, we demonstrated that our algorithm contains more profound conceptual incorporations and a broader semantic spectrum

contributions.statement: The AI Scientist — a fully automated, end-to-end scientific research pipeline that autonomously generates research ideas, writes and executes code, runs experiments, analyzes and plots results, writes complete machine-learning conference manuscripts, and performs automated peer review.
contributions.quote: Here we present a pipeline for automating the entire scientific process end to end. We present The AI Scientist, which creates research ideas, writes code, runs experiments, plots and analyses data, writes the entire scientific manuscript, and performs its own peer review.
contributions.quote_verbatim:

performs its own peer revie

contributions.statement: A template-free, open-ended research execution mode that uses agentic tree search to generate code from scratch, optimize implementations, and explore scientific hypotheses without human-provided scaffolding.
contributions.quote: template-free, open-ended mode that leverages agentic search for wider scientific exploration … The AI Scientist can generate an initial starting code script by itself. In this case, experimentation includes further stages for optimizing the code it writes from scratch, and experiment execution leverages extra test-time compute with a tree search

contributions.statement: The Automated Reviewer — an ensemble-based LLM reviewer that predicts conference acceptance decisions with accuracy on par with human reviewers, validated against openly available decisions from past conferences.
contributions.quote: we created an automated reviewer and first evaluated its performance against real, human-generated papers. The Automated Reviewer can accurately predict conference acceptance decisions, performing on par with human reviewers
contributions.quote_verbatim:

against real, human-generated papers. The Automated Reviewer can 
accurately predict conference acceptance decisions, performing on par 
with human reviewe

contributions.statement: AI-generated research manuscripts can pass the first round of peer review at a workshop of a top-tier machine learning conference, demonstrating that fully autonomous scientific output meets a non-trivial human-evaluated quality bar.
contributions.quote: Its ideas, execution and presentation are of sufficient quality that the manuscript generated by this AI system passed the first round of peer review for a workshop of a top-tier machine learning conference.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
ts ideas, execution and presentation are of sufficient 
quality that the manuscript generated by this AI system passed the first round of peer 
review for a workshop of a top-tier machine learning conference

contributions.statement: The quality of fully automated AI research papers scales with the capability of the underlying foundation model, with statistically significant improvement correlated with model release date.
contributions.quote: improvements to the base models significantly improve the quality of the generated papers, a finding that strongly implies that future versions of our system will be substantially more capable, as models continue to improve
contributions.quote_verbatim:

contributions.statement: A diagnostic framework identifying four methodological failure modes in AI scientist systems — inappropriate benchmark selection, data leakage, metric misuse, and post-hoc selection bias — with controlled experimental designs that isolate each failure mode using fully synthetic tasks to avoid data contamination.
contributions.quote: we identify four potential failure modes in contemporary AI scientist systems: inappropriate benchmark selection, data leakage, metric misuse, and post-hoc selection bias. To examine these risks, we design controlled experiments that isolate each failure mode while addressing challenges unique to evaluating AI scientist systems.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we identify four potential failure modes in contemporary AI scientist systems: inappropriate benchmark selection, data leakage, metric misuse, and post-hoc selection bias. To examine these risks, we design controlled experiments that isolate each failure mode while addressing challenges unique to evaluating AI scientist systems.

contributions.statement: Empirical evidence that prominent open-source AI scientist systems exhibit systematic methodological failures including benchmark cherry-picking, undocumented dataset manipulation, arbitrary metric selection, and post-hoc selection bias driven by internal reward mechanisms that exploit test-set performance.
contributions.quote: Our assessment of two prominent open-source AI scientist systems reveals the presence of several failures, across a spectrum of severity, which can be easily overlooked in practice.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Our assessment of two prominent open-source AI scientist systems reveals the presence of several failures, across a spectrum of severity, which can be easily overlooked in practice.

contributions.statement: An LLM-based auditing classifier for detecting methodological pitfalls in AI-generated research achieves substantially higher accuracy (82% binary classification, F1 0.81) when applied to workflow trace logs and generated code than when applied to the final paper alone (55% accuracy, F1 0.51).
contributions.quote: Using only the final paper as the evaluation target misses the opportunity for finding many critical failure modes… Access to log traces substantially enhances detection accuracy for pitfalls such as inappropriate benchmark selection, post-hoc selection bias, and metric misuse. The inclusion of generated code also strengthens this capability… (Binary classification accuracy 82%, F1 score 0.81.)
contributions.quote_verbatim: l failure modes, particularly those involving decision-making processes during experimentation. (Binary classification accuracy 55%, F1 score 0.51.) • Access to log traces substantially enhances detection accuracy for pitfalls such as inappropriate benchmark selection, post-hoc selection bias, and metric misuse. The inclusion of generated code also strengthens this capability, particularly for

contributions.statement: OpenReviewer, Papers with Reviews, and Reviewer Arena — three integrated AI review systems — enable automatic LLM-based peer review, public access to generated reviews, and pairwise preference-based evaluation of competing reviewer models.
contributions.quote: Three AI review systems: (i) OpenReviewer for automatic peer review with LLMs; (ii) Papers with Reviews an online paper review platform; and (iii) Reviewer Arena for evaluating reviewers by preferences.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Three AI review systems: (i) OpenReviewer for automatic peer review with LLMs; (ii) Papers with Reviews an online paper review platform; and (iii) Reviewer Arena for evaluating reviewers by preferences.

contributions.statement: A fine-tuned LLM trained on pairwise human preference data predicts which LLM-generated academic reviews humans will prefer in head-to-head comparisons, enabling scalable, low-cost proxy evaluation of review quality.
contributions.quote: we fine-tune an LLM to predict human preferences, predicting which reviews humans will prefer in a head-to-head battle between LLMs
contributions.quote_verbatim: we fine-tune an LLM to predict human preferences, predicting which reviews humans will prefer in a head-to-head battle between LLMs

contributions.statement: Artificially injecting errors and shortcomings into papers and systematically analyzing LLM reviewer responses provides an automatic method for discovering limitations and blind spots in LLM-based academic reviewing.
contributions.quote: Automatic discovery of LLM review limitations, using synthesis and analysis to map errors and shortcomings in LLM based reviewing
contributions.quote_verbatim: Automatic discovery of LLM review limitations, using synthesis and analysis to map errors and shortcomings in LLM based reviewing

contributions.statement: A role-playing framework in which multiple LLMs enact different peer-review roles (author, reviewer, area chair) in a multi-turn dialogue generates more specific and contextually grounded paper reviews than single-agent prompting.
contributions.quote: Role playing: Dialogue between LLMs playing different roles in the review process.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Role playing: Dialogue between LLMs playing different roles in the review process.

contributions.statement: A large-scale public corpus of LLM-generated reviews for arXiv and open-access Nature journal papers, paired with a free authoring service, makes merit-based, bias-mitigated academic reviews broadly accessible outside traditional conference pipelines.
contributions.quote: We make the reviews of publicly available arXiv and open-access Nature journal papers available online, along with a free service which helps authors review and revise their research papers and improve their quality.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We make the reviews of publicly available arXiv and open-access Nature journal papers available online, along with a free service which helps authors review and revise their research papers and improve their quality.

contributions.statement: MLGym — a Gym-compatible environment and unified framework for developing and evaluating LLM agents on open-ended AI research tasks, enabling research on reinforcement learning, curriculum learning, and open-ended learning algorithms for training such agents.
contributions.quote: we (i) introduce MLGym, the first Gym environment for evaluating and developing AI Research Agents
contributions.quote_verbatim: e (i) introduce \mlgym , the first Gym environment for evaluating and developing AI Research Agent

contributions.statement: MLGym-Bench — a benchmark suite of 13 diverse, open-ended AI research tasks spanning computer vision, natural language processing, reinforcement learning, and game theory, designed to evaluate LLM agents on realistic multifaceted research workflows.
contributions.quote: (ii) release MLGym-Bench, a suite of diverse open-ended AI research tasks for evaluating LLM agents
contributions.quote_verbatim: ii) release \mlgym -Bench, a suite of diverse open-ended AI research tasks for evaluating LLM agent

contributions.statement: An Area Under the Profile (AUP) score — an evaluation metric adapted from optimization and AutoML literature — enables fair comparison of LLM agents across tasks with heterogeneous performance metrics.
contributions.quote: (iii) propose a new evaluation metric for comparing multiple agents on a variety of tasks
contributions.quote_verbatim: (iii) propose a new evaluation metric for comparing multiple agents on a variety of tasks

contributions.statement: Frontier LLMs (Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Llama-3.1 405B, GPT-4o, o1-preview, Gemini-1.5 Pro) can improve on given baselines primarily through hyperparameter tuning but do not generate novel hypotheses, algorithms, or architectures when evaluated on open-ended AI research tasks.
contributions.quote: (iv) extensively evaluate frontier LLMs on MLGym-Bench. We find that current frontier models can improve on the given baselines, usually by finding better hyperparameters, but do not generate novel hypotheses, algorithms, architectures, or substantial improvements.
contributions.quote_verbatim: ng algorithms for training agents on AI research tasks. We find that current frontier models can improve on the given baselines, usually by finding better hyperparameters, but do not generate novel hypotheses, algorithms, architectures, or substantial improvements.

contributions.statement: A six-level capability taxonomy for AI Research Agents — ranging from reproduction of existing results to long-term autonomous research agenda — provides a structured framework for categorizing and targeting the autonomy and scientific contribution of LLM-based research agents.
contributions.quote: We propose a hierarchical framework to categorize the capabilities of LLM agents for accelerating AI research. This framework consists of six levels, each representing a distinct degree of autonomy and scientific contribution.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We propose a hierarchical framework to categorize the capabilities of LLM agents for accelerating AI research. This framework consists of six levels, each representing a distinct degree of autonomy and scientific contribution.

contributions.statement: Popper — an agentic framework for automated, statistically rigorous validation of free-form natural-language hypotheses using LLM agents that design and execute sequential falsification experiments.
contributions.quote: we propose POPPER, an agentic framework for rigorous automated validation of free-form hypotheses. Guided by Karl Popper's principle of falsification, POPPER validates a hypothesis using LLM agents that design and execute falsification experiments targeting its measurable implications.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
ropose POPPER, an agentic framework for rigor-
ous automated validation of free-form hypotheses.
Guided by Karl Popper’s principle of falsifica-
tion, POPPER validates a hypothesis using LLM
agents that design and execute falsification exper-
iments targeting its measurable implication

contributions.statement: A sequential testing framework based on e-values that aggregates evidence from multiple, potentially dependent LLM-generated falsification tests while maintaining strict Type-I error control and anytime validity.
contributions.quote: A novel sequential testing framework ensures strict Type-I error control while actively gathering evidence from diverse observations, whether drawn from existing data or newly conducted procedures.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
novel sequential testing framework ensures strict
Type-I error control while actively gathering evi-
dence from diverse observations, whether drawn
from existing data or newly conducted procedures.

contributions.statement: Automated hypothesis validation matching human PhD-level scientist performance across complex biological hypotheses while reducing validation time by an order of magnitude (10×).
contributions.quote: compared to human scientists, POPPER achieved comparable performance in validating complex biological hypotheses while reducing time by 10 folds, providing a scalable, rigorous solution for hypothesis validation.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
ompared to human scien-
tists, POPPER achieved comparable performance
in validating complex biological hypotheses while
reducing time by 10 folds, providing a scalable,
rigorous solution for hypothesis validation

contributions.statement: A substantial fraction (24%) of LLM-generated research documents are plagiarized — either paraphrased with one-to-one methodological mapping or significantly borrowed from existing work without attribution — as identified through an expert-led evaluation in which 13 domain experts actively searched for similarities across 50 LLM-generated research documents, with findings cross-verified by authors of the source papers.
contributions.quote: we conduct an expert-led evaluation where participants are instead instructed to presume plagiarism and actively search for it in LLM-generated research documents … experts identify 24% of the 50 evaluated research documents to be either paraphrased (with one-to-one methodological mapping), or significantly borrowed from existing work

be either paraphrased (with one-to-one method-
ological mapping), or significantly borrowed

contributions.statement: A synthetic dataset of research proposals intentionally plagiarized from existing papers enables controlled, systematic evaluation of automated plagiarism detection methods on idea-level plagiarism.
contributions.quote: To systematically evaluate automated detection methods, we create a synthetic dataset of research proposals intentionally plagiarized from existing papers.

contributions.statement: Automated plagiarism detection methods — including Semantic Scholar Augmented Generation (SSAG), embedding-based search (OpenScholar), and commercial text-matching tools (Turnitin) — are inadequate at catching idea-level plagiarism in LLM-generated research proposals.
contributions.quote: we evaluate common automated detection methods, including SSAG, OpenScholar (embedding-based search) (Asai et al., 2024), and a commercial service (Turnitin, LLC, 2025), and find them inadequate for detecting plagiarism in LLM-generated research proposals

research proposals

contributions.statement: LLM-generated research content is less diverse and follows more predictable patterns than human-written research, and these patterns may be detectable through basic classification methods to flag content for additional review.
contributions.quote: Our analysis in §6 also finds that LLM-generated research content is less diverse and follows more predictable patterns. Our preliminary investigation suggests these patterns might be detectable through basic classification methods, potentially helpful in flagging content for additional review
