contributions.statement: In a distributed multi-agent run on a trans-Pacific higher-order Kuramoto synchronization problem, agents autonomously identified and corrected a closure-ratio assumption in Ott–Antonsen analytic theory that fails outside the Lorentzian limit within thirty minutes, demonstrating self-correcting scientific reasoning without human intervention.
contributions.quote: In a trans-Pacific higher-order Kuramoto synchronization study, agents autonomously identified and corrected a closure-ratio assumption in Ott–Antonsen analytic theory that fails outside the Lorentzian limit, within thirty minutes.
contributions.quote_verbatim: In a trans-Pacific higher-order Kuramoto synchronization study, agents identified and corrected a closure-ratio assumption in Ott-Antonsen analytic theory that fails outside the Lorentzian limit, within thirty minutes. In an eight-

contributions.statement: An eight-agent heterogeneous-capability run on the 4.88M-cell Kang 2024 pan-cancer single-cell atlas, operating for 64.9 hours under a single structural external instruction, produced three new result layers and anchored findings against an independent wet-lab study on an adjacent CCR8⁻TIGIT⁺ Treg subset, demonstrating long-horizon emergent scientific coupling across diverse agent types.
contributions.quote: In an eight-agent single-cell run on the 4.88M-cell Kang 2024 pan-cancer atlas, heterogeneous capabilities coupled over a 64.9-hour window with exactly one structural external instruction, producing three new layers of result and anchoring findings against an independent wet-lab study on an adjacent CCR8- TIGIT+ Treg subset.
contributions.quote_verbatim: inutes. In an eight-agent single-cell run on the 4.88M-cell Kang 2024 pan-cancer atlas, heterogeneous capabilities coupled over a 64.9-hour window with one structural external instruction, producing three new result layers and anchoring findings against an independent wet-lab study on an adjacent CCR8- TIGIT+ Treg subset. Th

contributions.statement: ForeSci — a temporally controlled benchmark of 500 forward-looking AI research decision tasks spanning four AI domains and four decision families (direction forecasting, bottleneck–opportunity discovery, strategic research planning, venue-conditioned positioning), each paired with a cutoff-aligned offline knowledge base and pre-cutoff backbones, with post-cutoff papers withheld during generation and used only for validation; the same construction pipeline supports fully prospective forecasting as new literature emerges.
contributions.quote: A temporally-controlled benchmark with 500 tasks across four AI domains and four decision families, paired with cutoff-aligned offline knowledge bases and pre-cutoff backbones; the same pipeline supports fully prospective forecasting beyond retrospective evaluation
contributions.quote_verbatim: A temporally-controlled benchmark with 500 tasks across four AI domains and four decision families, paired with cutoff-aligned offline knowledge bases and pre-cutoff backbones; the same pipeline supports fully prospective forecasting beyond retrospective evaluation

contributions.statement: A multi-signal evaluation protocol for forward-looking research judgement that separately measures prediction factuality, future-target alignment, evidence traceability, and reviewer persuasiveness, validated against human experts.
contributions.quote: A multi-signal evaluation protocol separating factuality, future-target alignment, evidence traceability, and reviewer persuasiveness, validated against human experts.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A multi-signal evaluation protocol separating factuality, future-target alignment, evidence traceability, and reviewer persuasiveness, validated against human experts.

contributions.statement: Across LLM research agents, agent-style methods improve evidence traceability and factual support task-conditionally, but exhibit a recurring evidence-decision decoupling failure mode in which agents cite relevant pre-cutoff evidence while forecasting the wrong research object, mis-assigning causal roles, or selecting the wrong intervention.
contributions.quote: A systematic evaluation and diagnostic audit of LLM research agents showing that agent-style methods improve traceability and factuality task-conditionally, and identifying a previously-unstudied failure mode—evidence-decision decoupling.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A systematic evaluation and diagnostic audit of LLM research agents showing that agent-style methods improve traceability and factuality task-conditionally, and identifying a previously-unstudied failure mode—evidence-decision decoupling.

contributions.statement: A corpus of 37,802 AI-generated scientific ideas produced by four research-agent frameworks (zero-shot, AIScientist, ResearchAgent, AgentLaboratory) paired with six LLMs across citation-defined research areas in AI and machine learning, enabling systematic comparison of AI and human scientific ideation.
contributions.quote: Using four AI research-agent frameworks and six large language models, we generate 37,802 scientific ideas from shared seed literature across citation-defined research areas in AI and machine learning.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Using four AI research-agent frameworks and six large language models, we generate 37,802 scientific ideas from shared seed literature across citation-defined research areas in AI and machine learning.

contributions.statement: AI-generated scientific ideas are substantially more concentrated in topic space than human-authored papers from the same research areas, indicating that scale of AI ideation does not translate to breadth of scientific exploration.
contributions.quote: AI-generated ideas are substantially more concentrated than human-authored papers from the same research areas.
contributions.quote_verbatim: AI-generated ideas are substantially more concentrated than human-authored papers from the same research areas.

contributions.statement: AI-generated ideas remain much closer to their seed literature than human follow-on work does, staying within the conceptual neighborhood of input papers rather than moving into new research territory.
contributions.quote: AI-generated ideas remain much closer to their starting literature than later human follow-on work does. Rather than moving substantially beyond the seed literature, AI-generated ideas largely stay within the conceptual neighborhood of the input seed papers.
contributions.quote_verbatim: AI-generated ideas remain much closer to their starting literature than later human follow-on work does. Rather than moving substantially beyond the seed literature, AI-generated ideas largely stay within the conceptual neighborhood of the input seed papers.

contributions.statement: Papers most similar to AI-generated research ideas tend to receive fewer subsequent citations, suggesting AI agents preferentially occupy lower-impact regions of the scientific landscape.
contributions.quote: papers most similar to AI-generated ideas tend to receive lower subsequent citations.
contributions.quote_verbatim: papers most similar to AI-generated ideas tend to receive lower subsequent citations.

contributions.statement: When AI-generated ideas diverge from prior work, the divergence arises primarily from recombining existing technical methods rather than from posing fundamentally new research questions.
contributions.quote: when AI-generated ideas differ from prior work, the differences arise primarily from recombining existing technical methods rather than introducing fundamentally new research questions.
contributions.quote_verbatim: when AI-generated ideas differ from prior work, the differences arise primarily from recombining existing technical methods rather than introducing fundamentally new research questions.

contributions.statement: AutoScientists — a decentralized multi-agent system for long-running computational scientific experimentation in which agents interpret a shared experimental state, self-organize dynamically into teams around promising hypotheses, filter proposals through peer critique before committing experimental compute, and propagate successes and failures across teams to reduce redundant exploration — without any central orchestrator or pre-specified search decomposition.
contributions.quote: We introduce AutoScientists, a self-organizing agent team for long-horizon scientific experimentation. Unlike prior systems that rely on central coordinators, consensus-based discussion, or fixed decompositions of the search space, AutoScientists allows agents to independently interpret a shared experimental state and decide which hypotheses to pursue. Agents post proposals to a shared forum where peers critique and filter them before experiments run, allowing teams and experimental directions to emerge through interaction rather than external assignment.
contributions.quote_verbatim: rize our contributions: • A self-organizing agent team for long-horizon scientific experimentation . Unlike prior systems that rely on central coordinators, consensus-based discussion, or fixed decompositions of the search space, AutoScientists allows agents to independently interpret a shared experimental state and decide which hypotheses to pursue. Agents post proposals to a shared forum where peers critique and filter them before experiments run, allowing teams and experimental directions to emerge through interaction rather than external assignment. •

contributions.statement: Decentralized self-organizing agent teams outperform single-agent and centrally-coordinated multi-agent baselines on long-horizon scientific experimentation: on BioML-Bench (24 biomedical ML tasks) AutoScientists achieves a mean leaderboard percentile of 74.4%, improving the strongest prior AI agent by +8.33%; on GPT training optimization it reaches a target validation bits-per-byte 1.9× faster and discovers 7 accepted improvements where the single-agent baseline finds none; and on ProteinGym supervised substitution it improves average Spearman correlation by +6.5% over the prior state of the art across all 217 assays.
contributions.quote: State-of-the-art performance across scientific domains, with sustained improvement during long-running experimental search. AutoScientists improves over prior agents on biomedical ML, protein fitness prediction, and language-model training optimization, and continues identifying productive modifications after single-agent baselines stop improving.
contributions.quote_verbatim: State-of-the-art performance across scientific domains, with sustained improvement during long-running experimental search. AutoScientists improves over prior agents on biomedical ML, protein fitness prediction, and language-model training optimization, and continues identifying productive modifications after single-agent baselines stop improving.

contributions.statement: An automated-discovery extension to the Kermut protein fitness model for ACE2–Spike binding prediction achieves a +12.5% improvement in Spearman correlation over the prior state-of-the-art model, and the same frozen recipe transfers across all 217 ProteinGym supervised substitution assays to yield a +6.5% gain in average Spearman correlation.
contributions.quote: On ProteinGym fitness prediction, AutoScientists discovers a method for ACE2-Spike binding that improves over the current state-of-the-art model by +12.5% in Spearman correlation. Applied without modification across all 217 ProteinGym assays, the same method improves over the prior state of the art by +6.5% (Spearman correlation).
contributions.quote_verbatim: On ProteinGym fitness prediction, AutoScientists discovers a method for ACE2-Spike binding that improves over the current state-of-the-art model by +12.5% in Spearman correlation. Applied without modification across all 217 ProteinGym assays, the same method improves over the prior state of the art by +6.5% (Spearman correlation).

contributions.statement: InnovatorBench — a benchmark of 20 end-to-end LLM research tasks spanning data construction, filtering, augmentation, loss design, reward design, and scaffold construction — enables realistic, multi-dimensional evaluation of AI research agents on correctness, performance, output quality, and uncertainty, rewarding innovation over simple replication.
contributions.quote: We introduce InnovatorBench, the first benchmark to systematically evaluate AI research agents on end-to-end LLM research tasks, spanning data construction, filtering, and augmentation, loss design, reward design, and scaffold construction under multiple dimensions.
contributions.quote_verbatim: e introduce InnovatorBench , the first benchmark to systematically evaluate AI research agents on end-to-end LLM research tasks, spanning data construction, filtering, and augmentation, loss design, reward design, and scaffold construction under multiple dimensions.

contributions.statement: ResearchGym — a general, extensible research environment — supports long-duration and distributed experiments, asynchronous execution, snapshot saving and loading, and a broad action space (terminal commands, file operations, web search, browsing) for realistic agentic research workflows.
contributions.quote: We develop ResearchGym, a general and extensible research environment supporting long-duration and distributed experiments, asynchronous execution, snapshot saving and loading, and a broad action space for realistic research workflows.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We develop ResearchGym , a general and extensible research environment supporting long-duration and distributed experiments, asynchronous execution, snapshot saving and loading, and a broad action space for realistic research workflows

contributions.statement: Frontier LLMs evaluated as ReAct agents on end-to-end LLM research tasks show promise on code-driven sub-tasks but consistently fail on fragile algorithm design and long-horizon decision making, exhibiting impatience, resource mismanagement, poor library selection, and template-based reasoning, requiring over 11 hours to reach best performance.
contributions.quote: We perform an empirical analysis of InnovatorBench across multiple leading LLMs, demonstrating its potential and weaknesses in handling real LLM research tasks.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We perform an empirical analysis of InnovatorBench across multiple leading LLMs, demonstrating its potential and weaknesses in handling real LLM research tasks.

contributions.statement: SciIntBench — an adversarial benchmark of 810 prompts (270 matched triplets) spanning ten responsible conduct of research (RCR) categories and three scientific domains, where each scenario is presented in Overt Adversarial, Covert Adversarial, and Benign variants to jointly evaluate misconduct refusal and helpfulness on legitimate requests.
contributions.quote: We introduce SciIntBench, a benchmark of 270 triplets (Overt Adversarial, Covert Adversarial, Benign; 810 prompts in total) across ten RCR categories and three scientific domains.
contributions.quote_verbatim: ntroduce SciIntBench , a benchmark of 270 triplets ( Overt Adversarial , Covert Adversarial , Benign ; 810 prompts in total) across ten RCR categories and three scientific domains

contributions.statement: LLM refusal of research misconduct is strongly framing-sensitive: models that reliably refuse overtly stated misconduct frequently comply when the identical violation is framed covertly, and are especially vulnerable when misconduct is presented as a pressure-driven shortcut.
contributions.quote: We show that LLM refusal of research misconduct is highly sensitive to framing: models that reliably refuse overt misconduct may comply when the same request is framed covertly.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We show that LLM refusal of research misconduct is highly sensitive to framing: models that reliably refuse overt misconduct may comply when the same request is framed covertly.

contributions.statement: Across 16 commercial and open-weight LLMs evaluated with an LLM-as-a-judge pipeline validated against human annotators, newer model generations refuse misconduct more reliably than older ones but remain vulnerable to covert framings, with the weakest refusal boundaries observed in the transparency, plagiarism, and fabrication RCR categories.
contributions.quote: We evaluate 16 commercial and open-weight LLMs from six providers from 2024 to 2026 using an LLM-as-a-judge pipeline validated against human annotators, and find that newer models tend to refuse misconduct more reliably but remain vulnerable to covert framings.
contributions.quote_verbatim: evaluate 16 commercial and open-weight LLMs from six providers from 2024 to 2026 using an LLM-as-a-judge pipeline validated against human annotators ( ), and find that newer models tend to refuse misconduct more reliably but remain vulnerable to covert framings

contributions.statement: A three-phase synthetic environment generation pipeline — topic sampling, HuggingFace-grounded dataset proposal, and SWE-agent-compatible code generation — automatically produces diverse machine learning research tasks without human supervision.
contributions.quote: we provide a novel synthetic environment generation pipeline targeting machine learning agents. Our pipeline automatically synthesizes machine learning challenges compatible with the SWE-agent framework, covering topic sampling, dataset proposal, and code generation.
contributions.quote_verbatim: e a novel synthetic environment generation pipeline targeting machine learning agents. Our pipeline automatically synthesizes machine learning challenges compatible with the SWE-agent yang2024sweagentagentcomputerinterfacesenable framework, covering topic sampling, d

contributions.statement: A self-debugging verification loop that iteratively feeds execution errors back into starter-code generation increases the validity and yield of automatically synthesized ML tasks.
contributions.quote: verified for higher quality with a self-debugging loop … If there is an error during the execution, we collect the errors and feed them back to the model in step 3 (starter code generation) … The iterative debug process can continue at most times.
contributions.quote_verbatim: If there is an error during the execution, we collect the errors and feed them back to the model in step 3 (starter code generation) with probability or restart from step 3 with probability . The iterative debug process can continue at most times.

contributions.statement: Supervised fine-tuning small language models (Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B) on teacher-generated trajectories from synthetic ML tasks raises their performance on the MLGym benchmark by 9% and 12% respectively on the AUP metric.
contributions.quote: The student models trained with our synthetic tasks achieve improved performance on MLGym rasing the AUP metric by 9% for Qwen3-4B and and 12% for Qwen3-8B.
contributions.quote_verbatim: The student models trained with our synthetic tasks achieve improved performance on MLGym rasing the AUP metric by 9% for Qwen3-4B and and 12% for Qwen3-8B.

contributions.statement: SciAgentGym — a scalable, stateful interactive environment integrating 1,780 domain-specific tools across Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Materials Science, with supporting infrastructure (filesystem, scientific databases, Python interpreter) for grounding LLM agents in multi-turn scientific tool-use reasoning.
contributions.quote: SciAgentGym: An extensible environment integrating 1,780 domain-specific tools across four scientific disciplines to ground agents in multi-turn reasoning.
contributions.quote_verbatim: SciAgentGym : An extensible environment integrating 1,780 domain-specific tools across four scientific disciplines to ground agents in multi-turn reasoning

contributions.statement: SciAgentBench — a tiered evaluation suite of 259 tasks and 1,134 sub-questions spanning elementary single-tool actions (L1) to long-horizon multi-step workflows (L3), designed to isolate where agents fail in complex scientific problem-solving.
contributions.quote: SciAgentBench: An evaluation suite spanning elementary actions to long-horizon workflows, designed to quantify the gap between tool availability and mastery.
contributions.quote_verbatim: ciAgentBench : An evaluation suite spanning elementary actions to long-horizon workflows, designed to quantify the gap between tool availability and mastery.

contributions.statement: SciForge — a data synthesis method that models a scientific tool collection as a dependency graph, samples valid execution paths, and generates logic-aware training trajectories grounded in verified runtime traces, enabling smaller fine-tuned models to outperform much larger models on scientific tool-use tasks.
contributions.quote: SciForge: A graph-based data synthesis method that generates logic-aware training trajectories, enabling our 8B model to outperform 200B+ scale models.
contributions.quote_verbatim: SciForge : A graph-based data synthesis method that generates logic-aware training trajectories, enabling our 8B model to outperform 200B+ scale models

contributions.statement: Fine-tuning an 8B model (SciAgent-8B) on execution-grounded, dependency-graph-sampled trajectories surpasses Qwen3-VL-235B-Instruct by +6.7% on scientific tool-use benchmarks and exhibits positive cross-domain transfer of scientific tool-use capabilities.
contributions.quote: By fine-tuning on these trajectories, our SciAgent-8B outperforms the significantly larger Qwen3-VL-235B-Instruct while exhibiting positive cross-domain transfer of scientific tool-use capabilities.
contributions.quote_verbatim: By fine-tuning on these trajectories, our SciAgent-8B outperforms the significantly larger Qwen3-VL-235B-Instruct while exhibiting positive cross-domain transfer of scientific tool-use capabilities.

contributions.statement: Medical AI Scientist — an end-to-end agentic framework for autonomous clinical research comprising an Idea Proposer, Experimental Executor, and Manuscript Composer — is the first autonomous research system explicitly tailored to clinical medicine, operating across three modes (paper-based reproduction, literature-inspired innovation, and task-driven exploration) with progressively increasing autonomy.
contributions.quote: we introduce Medical AI Scientist, the first autonomous research framework tailored to clinical autonomous research... The framework operates under 3 research modes, namely paper-based reproduction, literature-inspired innovation, and task-driven exploration, each corresponding to a distinct level of automated scientific inquiry with progressively increasing autonomy.
contributions.quote_verbatim: earch ideas. The Medical AI scientist further introduces evidence-grounded manuscript drafting guided by a structured medical writing paradigm and ethical policies. The framework operates under 3 research modes, namely paper-based reproduction, literature-inspired innovation, and task-driven exploration, corresponding to distinct levels of medical scientific autonomy.

contributions.statement: A clinician–engineer co-reasoning mechanism grounds autonomously generated medical research hypotheses in verifiable literature evidence, improving traceability and reducing hallucinations in clinical AI ideation.
contributions.quote: A clinician–engineer co-reasoning mechanism is incorporated into the idea generation process to explicitly ground each hypothesis in verifiable evidence and mitigate hallucinations.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A clinician–engineer co-reasoning mechanism is incorporated into the idea generation process to explicitly ground each hypothesis in verifiable evidence and mitigate hallucinations.

contributions.statement: A hierarchical manuscript composition approach guided by a structured medical writing paradigm and embedded ethical review mechanisms produces evidence-grounded clinical research drafts that comply with medical publication policies on data provenance and ethics.
contributions.quote: A hierarchical Manuscript Composer transforms research outputs into coherent and evidence-grounded drafts through a structured medical writing paradigm with enhanced narrative logic and readability. It also embeds ethical review mechanisms that explicitly document data usage in compliance with medical publication policies.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A hierarchical Manuscript Composer transforms research outputs into coherent and evidence-grounded drafts through a structured medical writing paradigm with enhanced narrative logic and readability. It also embeds ethical review mechanisms that explicitly document data usage in compliance with medical publication policies.

contributions.statement: Med-AI Bench — a benchmark of 171 evaluation cases spanning 19 clinical tasks and 6 medical data modalities — provides a standardized framework for assessing automated medical research systems across the full research pipeline (idea generation, experimental execution, and manuscript quality).
contributions.quote: we introduce Med-AI Bench... This benchmark comprises 171 high-quality evaluation cases, organized around 19 distinct research tasks spanning 6 common medical data modalities... This design provides a systematic and unified framework for both qualitative and quantitative assessment of automated medical research systems across the full research pipeline.
contributions.quote_verbatim: cal data modalities. For each task, we selected 3 representative papers of varying difficulty (easy, medium, hard) and constructed 3 evaluation cases with different input modes. This design provides a systematic and unified framework for both qualitative and quantitative assessment of automated medical research systems across the full research pipeline.

contributions.statement: EvoScientist — a self-evolving multi-agent framework comprising a Researcher Agent, an Engineer Agent, and an Evolution Manager Agent with two persistent memory modules (ideation memory and experimentation memory) — continuously improves scientific idea quality and code execution reliability by learning from accumulated successes and failures across end-to-end scientific discovery tasks.
contributions.quote: We propose EvoScientist, a self-evolving multi-agent system with three specialized agents and two persistent memory modules, aiming to improve both the quality of generated research ideas and the reliability of code generation and execution.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We propose EvoScientist, a self-evolving multi-agent system with three specialized agents and two persistent memory modules, aiming to improve both the quality of generated research ideas and the reliability of code generation and execution.

contributions.statement: Three multi-agent self-evolution mechanisms — idea direction evolution, idea validation evolution, and experiment strategy evolution — enable an AI scientist system to learn from accumulated outcomes and failures and to continuously improve both idea generation and experiment execution over time.
contributions.quote: We introduce three multi-agent self-evolution mechanisms, namely idea direction evolution, idea validation evolution, and experiment strategy evolution, that enable EvoScientist to learn from accumulated outcomes and failures and to continuously improve both idea generation and experiment execution over time.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introduce three multi-agent self-evolution mechanisms, namely idea direction evolution, idea validation evolution, and experiment strategy evolution, that enable EvoScientist to learn from accumulated outcomes and failures and to continuously improve both idea generation and experiment execution over time.

contributions.statement: A multi-agent evolving AI scientist system with persistent memory achieves higher novelty, feasibility, relevance, and clarity in scientific idea generation and higher code execution success rates compared to seven open-source and commercial state-of-the-art baselines, with all six end-to-end generated papers accepted to a peer-reviewed venue and two receiving awards.
contributions.quote: We provide empirical evidence that EvoScientist generates higher-quality ideas and achieves higher code execution success rates compared to strong open-source and commercial baselines.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We provide empirical evidence that EvoScientist generates higher-quality ideas and achieves higher code execution success rates compared to strong open-source and commercial baselines.

contributions.statement: aiXiv — an open-access, multi-agent platform with API and MCP interfaces — enables both human and AI scientists to submit, review, and iteratively refine scientific proposals and papers within a unified, extensible ecosystem.
contributions.quote: We introduce aiXiv, the first extensible infrastructure that enables seamless collaboration between AI agents and human researchers for generating, refining, and disseminating scientific proposals and papers. The platform provides APIs and MCPs interfaces for uploading, retrieving, reviewing and discussing scientific proposals and papers.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introduce aiXiv, the first extensible infrastructure that enables seamless collaboration between AI agents and human researchers for generating, refining, and disseminating scientific proposals and papers. The platform provides APIs and MCPs interfaces for uploading, retrieving, reviewing and discussing scientific proposals and papers.

contributions.statement: A closed-loop review pipeline featuring automatic retrieval-augmented evaluation, reviewer guidance, multi-voting publication decisions, and a five-stage prompt injection detection and defense mechanism for AI-reviewed scientific submissions.
contributions.quote: We develop a closed-loop review system for both proposals and papers, featuring automatic retrieval-augmented evaluation, reviewer guidance, and defense mechanisms against prompt injection.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We develop a closed-loop review system for both proposals and papers, featuring automatic retrieval-augmented evaluation, reviewer guidance, and defense mechanisms against prompt injection.

contributions.statement: Curated datasets for benchmarking scientific proposal quality and evaluating AI-driven review effectiveness in the context of AI-generated research content.
contributions.quote: We also release curated datasets for benchmarking proposal quality and evaluating review effectiveness.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We also release curated datasets for benchmarking proposal quality and evaluating review effectiveness.

contributions.statement: Iterative AI-driven review-and-refine cycles yield measurable improvements in the quality of AI-generated research proposals and papers, with gains in proposal ranking, review helpfulness, and final paper quality.
contributions.quote: Through comprehensive experiments on real-world scientific topics, we show that our review-refine pipeline substantially improves the quality of AI-generated research content. Iterative reviews yield measurable gains in proposal ranking, review helpfulness, and final paper quality.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Through comprehensive experiments on real-world scientific topics, we show that our review-refine pipeline substantially improves the quality of AI-generated research content. Iterative reviews yield measurable gains in proposal ranking, review helpfulness, and final paper quality.

contributions.statement: OpenLens AI — a modular, fully automated research agent framework for health informatics — integrates specialized agents for literature review, data analysis, code generation, and manuscript preparation into an end-to-end pipeline that requires no human intervention.
contributions.quote: A modular agent architecture tailored to health informatics, featuring specialized agents for literature review, data analysis, code generation, and manuscript preparation.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A modular agent architecture tailored to health informatics, featuring specialized agents for literature review, data analysis, code generation, and manuscript preparation.

contributions.statement: Vision-language feedback integrated into an automated research pipeline enables iterative evaluation and refinement of medical visualizations (plots, charts, curves), closing a gap left by general-purpose research agents.
contributions.quote: Integration of vision-language feedback mechanisms to evaluate and refine visual outputs, including plots, charts, and curves—addressing a critical gap in current research agents.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Integration of vision-language feedback mechanisms to evaluate and refine visual outputs, including plots, charts, and curves—addressing a critical gap in current research agents.

contributions.statement: A quality control framework embedded in an autonomous health informatics agent enforces methodological soundness, statistical reliability, and reproducibility, reducing the likelihood of misleading or hallucinated findings.
contributions.quote: A quality control framework that supports methodological soundness, statistical reliability, and reproducibility, reducing the likelihood of misleading results.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A quality control framework that supports methodological soundness, statistical reliability, and reproducibility, reducing the likelihood of misleading results.

contributions.statement: End-to-end automation of the health informatics research pipeline — from initial ideation through literature review, data analysis, and code execution to publication-ready LaTeX manuscript generation — is achievable with transparent and traceable workflows.
contributions.quote: End-to-end automation of the health informatics research pipeline, from initial ideation to publication-ready LaTeX manuscripts.
contributions.quote_verbatim: End-to-end automation of the health informatics research pipeline, from initial ideation to publication-ready LaTeX manuscripts.

contributions.statement: STELLA — a self-evolving multi-agent system for biomedical research whose capabilities grow autonomously through a dynamically updated Template Library of successful reasoning workflows and an expanding Tool Ocean of bioinformatics tools discovered and integrated by a dedicated Tool Creation Agent.
contributions.quote: Here, we introduce STELLA, a self-evolving AI agent designed to overcome these limitations. STELLA employs a multi-agent architecture that autonomously improves its own capabilities through two core mechanisms: an evolving Template Library for reasoning strategies and a dynamic Tool Ocean that expands as a Tool Creation Agent automatically discovers and integrates new bioinformatics tools.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Here, we introduce STELLA, a self-evolving AI agent designed to overcome these limitations. STELLA employs a multi-agent architecture that autonomously improves its own capabilities through two core mechanisms: an evolving Template Library for reasoning strategies and a dynamic Tool Ocean that expands as a Tool Creation Agent automatically discovers and integrates new bioinformatics tools.

contributions.statement: A Tool Creation Agent mechanism that autonomously identifies capability gaps, searches for relevant bioinformatics tools and APIs, and integrates them into a live tool repository, enabling an agent's toolset to expand at inference time without manual curation.
contributions.quote: the Tool Creation Agent can autonomously identify, test, and integrate new tools in response to the demands of a novel problem, ensuring its capabilities are never limited to a predefined set
contributions.quote_verbatim: The Tool Creation Agent can autonomously identify, test, and integrate new tools in response to the demands of a novel problem, ensuring its capabilities are never limited to a predefined set

contributions.statement: Self-evolving test-time scaling in biomedical AI: iteratively running a multi-agent system on the same benchmark with increasing computational trials nearly doubles accuracy (14 % → 26 % on Humanity's Last Exam: Biomedicine), demonstrating systematic performance improvement through accumulated reasoning experience.
contributions.quote: This improvement is substantial; with increased computational experience, STELLA's accuracy on HLE: Biomedicine benchmark almost doubles, rising from 14% to 26%
contributions.quote_verbatim: This improvement is substantial; with increased computational experience, STELLA’s accuracy on HLE: Biomedicine benchmark almost doubles, rising from 14% to 26%

contributions.statement: State-of-the-art accuracy on biomedical question-answering benchmarks: ~26 % on Humanity's Last Exam: Biomedicine, ~54 % on LAB-Bench DBQA, and ~63 % on LAB-Bench LitQA, outperforming leading LLMs and specialized agents by up to 8 percentage points.
contributions.quote: STELLA consistently establishes a new state of the art, achieving top accuracy scores of approximately 26% on Humanity's Last Exam: Biomedicine, 54% on LAB-Bench: DBQA, and 63% on LAB-Bench: LitQA, outperforming the next-best models by up to 8 percentage points
contributions.quote_verbatim: STELLA consistently establishes a new state of the art, achieving top accuracy scores of approximately 26% on Humanity’s Last Exam: Biomedicine, 54% on LAB-Bench: DBQA, and 63% on LAB-Bench: LitQA, outperforming the next-best models by up to 8 percentage points

contributions.statement: A benchmark of three controlled black-box reverse-engineering tasks — reconstructing list-mapping programs, formal languages, and math equations — designed to assess scientific discovery capabilities of LLMs under both passive observation and active intervention conditions.
contributions.quote: we formalize reverse-engineering as a core problem for assessing the scientific discovery capabilities of LLMs and design three black-box tasks that can be used in such assessment.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we formalize reverse-engineering as a core problem for assessing the scientific discovery capabilities of LLMs and design three black-box tasks that can be used in such assessment.

contributions.statement: Frontier LLMs fail to efficiently extract information from passively observed input-output data when reverse-engineering black-box systems, reaching a performance plateau that falls significantly short of Bayesian inference.
contributions.quote: We demonstrate empirically that frontier LLMs still struggle, relative to Bayesian inference, at reverse-engineering these black boxes when provided with only passive observations.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We demonstrate empirically that frontier LLMs still struggle, relative to Bayesian inference, at reverse-engineering these black boxes when provided with only passive observations.

contributions.statement: Allowing LLMs to actively intervene — self-generating targeted queries to a black-box and observing the outputs — improves reverse-engineering performance and helps escape two failure modes: overcomplication (assuming false prior knowledge) and overlooking (failing to incorporate observations).
contributions.quote: We show that LLMs can perform interventions to obtain more informative data, and that effective intervention mitigates the failure modes of overcomplication and overlooking.
contributions.quote_verbatim: e show that LLMs can perform interventions to obtain more informative data, and that effective intervention mitigates the failure modes of overcomplication and overlooking .

contributions.statement: The performance gain from LLM-generated interventions stems primarily from the act of generating those interventions (self-testing and refining beliefs) rather than from data quality alone: feeding one LLM's intervention data as passive observations to a second LLM degrades performance, paralleling findings on human active learning.
contributions.quote: We show that performance degrades when repurposing the LLM's intervention data as observations, pinpointing the mechanism behind the improvements it produces and highlighting a potential pitfall for exchanging knowledge among LLMs.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We show that performance degrades when repurposing the LLM’s intervention data as observations, pinpointing the mechanism behind the improvements it produces and highlighting a potential pitfall for exchanging knowledge among LLMs.

contributions.statement: El Agente is a cognitive architecture for LLM-based agents that integrates working memory, long-term memory, and an LLM reasoning core into a hierarchical cybernetic agent network, enabling hierarchical planning, long-term memory retrieval, and modular memory management for complex multi-step task execution.
contributions.quote: we introduce El Agente, a novel cognitive architecture for language agents, and apply it to automate complex computational and quantum chemistry tasks. El Agente employs a hierarchical network of specialized LLM-based agents, each equipped with an extensive list of available tools.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
e introduce El Agente,1 a novel cognitive architecture for language agents, and apply it to
automate complex computational and quantum chemistry tasks. We refer to this application of El Agente as
El Agente Q (Figure 1). El Agente employs a hierarchical network of specialized LLM-b

contributions.statement: El Agente Q is an LLM-based multi-agent system that dynamically generates and executes quantum chemistry workflows from natural language prompts, with capabilities including flexible task decomposition, adaptive tool selection, post-analysis, autonomous file handling, SLURM job submission, and in situ error debugging.
contributions.quote: we introduce El Agente Q, an LLM-based multi-agent system that dynamically generates and executes quantum chemistry workflows from natural language user prompts. The system is built on a novel cognitive architecture featuring a hierarchical memory framework that enables flexible task decomposition, adaptive tool selection, post-analysis, and autonomous file handling and submission.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
we introduce El Agente Q, an LLM-based multi-agent system that dynamically
generates and executes quantum chemistry workflows from natural language user prompts. The system
is built on a novel cognitive architecture featuring a hierarchical memory framework that enables
flexible task decomposition, adaptive tool selection, post-analysis, and autonomous file handling and
submission.

contributions.statement: An LLM-based multi-agent quantum chemistry system achieves greater than 87% average task success across six university-level course exercises and two research case studies, with adaptive error recovery via in situ debugging.
contributions.quote: El Agente Q is benchmarked on six university-level course exercises and two case studies, demonstrating robust problem-solving performance (averaging >87% task success) and adaptive error handling through in situ debugging.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
El Agente Q is benchmarked on six university-level course exercises and two case studies,
demonstrating robust problem-solving performance (averaging >87% task success) and adaptive error
handling through in situ debugging.

contributions.statement: Exporting agent action traces as executable code provides a transparency mechanism that lets human experts verify procedural correctness and refine dynamically generated computational workflows.
contributions.quote: A key feature is its ability to extract the action traces of the agents involved in its dynamic workflows, which can be exported as code to verify the correctness of the procedure or assist humans in refined workflow design.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
A key feature is its ability to extract the action traces of
the agents involved in its dynamic workflows, which can be exported as code to verify the correctness of the
procedure or assist humans in refined workflow design.

contributions.statement: A standardized comparative benchmark evaluating eight free-version AI chatbots—ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, DeepSeek, Gemini, Grok, Le Chat, and Perplexity—on the accuracy of academic bibliographic reference generation across five major knowledge domains (Health, Engineering, Experimental Sciences, Social Sciences, and Humanities), auditing 400 references against five formal criteria (authorship, year, title, source, and location).
contributions.quote: The novelty of this study lies in offering the first comparative analysis of eight AI chatbots specifically focused on their ability to generate bibliographic references for student academic work.

contributions.statement: Only 26.5% of bibliographic references generated by current AI chatbots are fully accurate, while 33.8% are partially correct and 39.8% are erroneous or entirely fabricated, revealing a critical reliability gap for academic use.
contributions.quote: The results show that only 26.5% of the references were fully correct, 33.8% partially correct, and 39.8% were either erroneous or entirely fabricated.

contributions.statement: Grok and DeepSeek are the only chatbots among eight tested that produce zero hallucinated bibliographic references, whereas Copilot, Perplexity, and Claude exhibit the highest hallucination rates.
contributions.quote: Grok and DeepSeek stood out as the only chatbots that did not generate false references, while Copilot, Perplexity, and Claude exhibited the highest hallucination rates.

contributions.statement: AI chatbots show a systematic bias toward generating book references over journal articles, yet journal article citations carry a significantly higher fabrication rate than book citations.
contributions.quote: the chatbots showed a greater tendency to generate book references over journal articles, although the latter had a significantly higher fabrication rate.

contributions.statement: Substantial overlap exists in the bibliographic sources recommended by different AI chatbot models, particularly among DeepSeek, Grok, Gemini, and ChatGPT, indicating convergent generative behavior across ostensibly independent systems.
contributions.quote: A high degree of overlap was also detected among the sources provided by several models, particularly between DeepSeek, Grok, Gemini, and ChatGPT.

contributions.statement: AstaBench — a benchmark suite of 2400+ problems spanning the full scientific discovery process and multiple scientific domains, including problems derived from real user requests to deployed AI research assistants, with a standardized task interface for general-purpose agents.
contributions.quote: we present AstaBench, a suite that provides a holistic measure of agentic ability to perform scientific research, comprising 2400+ problems spanning the entire scientific discovery process and multiple scientific domains, and including many problems inspired by actual user requests to deployed Asta agents
contributions.quote_verbatim:
we present AstaBench, a suite that provides a
holistic measure of agentic ability to perform scientific research, comprising 2400+
problems spanning the entire scientific discovery process and multiple scientific
domains, and including many problems inspired by actual user requests to deployed
Asta agents

contributions.statement: The Asta Environment — the first agent evaluation environment providing production-grade scientific literature search tools that enable controlled, reproducible agent evaluation while isolating core agentic capabilities from information-access advantages.
contributions.quote: AstaBench includes the powerful Asta Environment, the first agent environment that enables controlled, reproducible evaluation with production-grade search tools for retrieving information from a large corpus of scientific literature
contributions.quote_verbatim:
h includes the powerful Asta Environment (Section 4), the first agent environment that
enables controlled, reproducible evaluation with production-grade search tools for retrieving
information from a large corpus of scientific litera

contributions.statement: The agent-eval Agents Evaluation Toolkit — an open-source toolkit for defining benchmark suites and leaderboards with time-invariant cost accounting using logged model usage, enabling rigorous confounder-controlled agent comparison.
contributions.quote: We also introduce the agent-eval Agents Evaluation Toolkit, which enables defining a benchmark suite and leaderboard with time-invariant cost accounting using model usages logged by Inspect
contributions.quote_verbatim:
also introduce the agent-eval Agents Evaluation Toolkit3 (Section 4.2), which enables
defining a benchmark suite and leaderboard with time-invariant cost accounting using model usages
logge

contributions.statement: The first agent leaderboard that explicitly accounts for confounding variables — including tools used and inference cost — when reporting and comparing agent performance.
contributions.quote: It's the first agent leaderboard to properly account for confounding variables such as the tools used by the agent and inference cost
contributions.quote_verbatim:
It’s the first agent leaderboard to
properly account for confounding variables such as the tools used by the agent and inference cost

contributions.statement: A comprehensive standardized suite of nine science-optimized Asta agent classes plus numerous baselines, enabling meaningful comparison of 57 agents across 22 agent architectures on scientific research tasks.
contributions.quote: we present the agent-baselines Agents Suite, the most comprehensive standardized agents suite, comprised of nine Asta agent classes that have been optimized for scientific research tasks, as well as numerous baselines
contributions.quote_verbatim:
t the agent-baselines Agents Suite5 (Section 4.3), the most comprehensive
standardized agents suite, comprised of nine Asta agent classes that have been optimized for
scientific research tasks, as well as numerous bas

contributions.statement: Reinforcement Learning with Evolving Rubrics (RLER) — a training method where per-instance rubrics are initialized from search-retrieved evidence and co-evolve with the policy model by incorporating newly explored search results and contrastive model responses at each training step, providing on-policy, discriminative rewards for long-form tasks.
contributions.quote: We address this with Reinforcement Learning with Evolving Rubrics (RLER), where rubrics are constructed and maintained to co-evolve with the policy model during training. This allows the rubrics to incorporate newly explored information from search and contrasting model responses, enabling better fact checking and more discriminative on-policy feedback.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
e address this with Reinforcement Learning
with Evolving Rubrics (RLER), where rubrics
are constructed and maintained to co-evolve with
the policy model during training. This allows the
rubrics to incorporate newly explored informa-
tion from search and contrasting model responses,
enabling better fact checking and more discrimi-
native on-policy feedba

contributions.statement: DR Tulu-8B — the first fully open language model directly trained end-to-end for open-ended, long-form deep research, outperforming larger open deep research agents and matching proprietary systems at roughly 1000× lower cost per query.
contributions.quote: Using RLER, we develop Deep Research Tulu (DR Tulu-8B), the first fully open model that is directly trained for open-ended, long-form deep research.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
ing RLER, we de-
velop Deep Research Tulu (DR Tulu-8B), the
first fully open model that is directly trained for
open-ended, long-form deep research.

contributions.statement: GeneticDiseasesQA — a challenging clinical deep research dataset requiring models to search for and synthesize evidence to assess the therapeutic eligibility of disease-causing genetic variants, with verifiable citation requirements.
contributions.quote: We further construct GeneticDiseasesQA, a challenging clinical deep research dataset that requires models to search for and synthesize supporting evidence to assess the therapeutic eligibility of disease-causing genetic variants.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We further construct
GeneticDiseasesQA, a challenging clinical deep research
dataset that requires models to search for and synthesize
supporting evidence to assess the therapeutic eligibility of
disease-causing genetic variants.

contributions.statement: An end-to-end open-source deep research training stack — including data, code, models, an extensible deep research library (dr-agent-lib), and an evaluation suite supporting plug-and-play multi-tool search with infrastructure for asynchronous tool calls and scalable RL over long-horizon tool-use trajectories.
contributions.quote: We release all data, code, and models, along with an extensible deep research library (dr-agent-lib) and an evaluation suite supporting plug-and-play multi-tool search. This release provides an end-to-end training stack for deep research agents, including data and infrastructure for asynchronous tool calls and scalable RL over long-horizon tool-use trajectories.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
release all data, code, and models, along with an ex-
tensible deep research library (dr-agent-lib) and an
evaluation suite supporting plug-and-play multi-tool search.
This release provides an end-to-end training stack for deep
research agents, including data and infrastructure for asyn-
chronous tool calls and scalable RL over long-horizon tool-
use trajectori

contributions.statement: Training on open-ended deep research with RLER yields a 6.4–16.0 point gain over supervised fine-tuning alone and causes models to adaptively select among multiple search tools (e.g., paper search vs. web search) based on task domain rather than relying on a single hard-coded tool.
contributions.quote: Our analysis shows that RLER improves the model's ability to produce more comprehensive and in-depth long-form responses with accurate citations, yielding gains of 6.4–16.0 points on top of the finetuned model across the four benchmarks. Moreover, DR Tulu-8B learns to select appropriate search tools for each task, instead of relying on a single hard-coded search tool like in prior work.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Our analysis shows that RLER improves the model’s ability
to produce more comprehensive and in-depth long-form re-
sponses with accurate citations, yielding gains of 6.4–16.0
points on top of the finetuned model across the four bench-
marks. Moreover, DR Tulu-8B learns to select appropriate
search tools for each task, instead of relying on a single
hard-coded search tool like in prior w

contributions.statement: A large-scale benchmark evaluating LLM-based scientific agents across eight domains — spanning workflow execution to hypothesis-driven inquiry — through more than 25,000 agent runs, using two complementary lenses: systematic performance analysis and epistemological behavioral analysis of reasoning traces.
contributions.quote: we evaluate LLM-based scientific agents across eight domains, spanning workflow execution to hypothesis-driven inquiry, through more than 25,000 agent runs and two complementary lenses: (i) a systematic performance analysis that decomposes the contributions of the base model and the agent scaffold, and (ii) a behavioral analysis of the epistemological structure of agent reasoning
contributions.quote_verbatim:
we evaluate LLM-based scientific agents across eight domains, spanning workflow
execution to hypothesis-driven inquiry, through more than 25,000 agent runs and two
complementary lenses: (i) a systematic performance analysis that decomposes the
contributions of the base model and the agent scaffold, and (ii) a behavioral analysis of
the epistemological structure of agent reasoning

contributions.statement: Decomposing agent behavior into base-model and scaffold components reveals that the base model is the dominant driver of both task performance and epistemic behavior, accounting for 41.4% of explained variance versus only 1.5% for the scaffold.
contributions.quote: the base model is the primary determinant of both performance and behavior, accounting for 41.4% of explained variance versus 1.5% for the scaffold
contributions.quote_verbatim:
the base model is
the primary determinant of both performance and behavior, accounting for 41.4% of
explained variance versus 1.5% for the scaffold

contributions.statement: LLM-based scientific agents systematically fail to reason epistemically: evidence is ignored in 68% of reasoning traces, refutation-driven belief revision occurs in only 26%, and convergent multi-test evidence is rare — patterns that persist across both workflow-execution and hypothesis-driven tasks and even when near-complete successful trajectories are provided as context.
contributions.quote: evidence is ignored in 68% of traces, refutation-driven belief revision occurs in 26%, and convergent multi-test evidence is rare. The same reasoning pattern appears whether the agent executes a computational workflow or conducts hypothesis-driven inquiry. They persist even when agents receive near-complete successful reasoning trajectories as context
contributions.quote_verbatim:
evidence is
ignored in 68% of traces, refutation-driven belief revision occurs in 26%, and convergent
multi-test evidence is rare. The same reasoning pattern appears whether the agent
executes a computational workflow or conducts hypothesis-driven inquiry. They persist
even when agents receive near-complete successful reasoning trajectories as context

contributions.statement: Outcome-based evaluation of scientific agents cannot detect epistemic reasoning failures, and scaffold engineering alone is insufficient to repair them — progress requires targeting the reasoning process itself at the base-model training level.
contributions.quote: Outcome-based evaluation cannot detect these failures, and scaffold engineering alone cannot repair them. Until reasoning itself becomes a training target, the scientific knowledge produced by such agents cannot be justified by the process that generated it
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Outcome-
based evaluation cannot detect these failures, and scaffold engineering alone cannot
repair them. Until reasoning itself becomes a training target, the scientific knowledge
produced by such agents cannot be justified by the process that generated i

contributions.statement: DeepResearch Bench — a benchmark of 100 PhD-level research tasks spanning 22 distinct fields, constructed by domain experts to reflect the real-world distribution of deep research demands derived from over 96,000 user queries — enables systematic end-to-end evaluation of Deep Research Agents.
contributions.quote: We present DeepResearch Bench, the first specialized benchmark for evaluating Deep Research Agents, constructed through a systematic process that reflects authentic user needs.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We present DeepResearch Bench, the first specialized benchmark for evaluating Deep Research Agents, con-
structed through a systematic process that reflects authentic user need

contributions.statement: RACE (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation with Dynamic Weighting) — a report-quality evaluation framework that dynamically generates task-specific criteria and dimension weights, then scores a target report relative to a high-quality reference — achieves strong alignment with human judgment for assessing long-form research reports across diverse domains.
contributions.quote: We further propose RACE and FACT, two novel evaluation frameworks that respectively assess the report generation quality and the information retrieval abilities of Deep Research Agents.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We further propose RACE and FACT, two novel evaluation frameworks that respectively assess the report
generation quality and the information retrieval abilities of Deep Research Agents.

contributions.statement: FACT (Factual Abundance and Citation Trustworthiness) — a framework that evaluates information retrieval and collection capabilities of research agents by measuring effective citation count and overall citation accuracy — provides a robust, human-aligned approach for assessing the coverage and reliability of information gathered by LLM-based agents.
contributions.quote: The other framework is introduced to evaluate DRA's information retrieval and collection capabilities by assessing its effective citation count and overall citation accuracy.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
The other framework is introduced to evaluate
DRA’s information retrieval and collection capabilities by assessing its effective citation count and overall
citation accuracy.

contributions.statement: Comprehensive human studies confirm that RACE and FACT achieve strong alignment with human judgment, validating both frameworks as reliable automated evaluators for Deep Research Agents.
contributions.quote: We conduct comprehensive human studies to validate the reliability of our frameworks and publicly release the benchmark and evaluation protocols to foster future research.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We conduct comprehensive human studies to validate the reliability of our frameworks and publicly release the
benchmark and evaluation protocols to foster future research.

contributions.statement: Three explicit regulatory feedback loops — interpretive update (classifying incoming evidence against the current mental model), deviation feedback (scoring search outcomes against planner expectations to route corrective strategies), and schema revision (restructuring concept labels, relations, and inquiry goals when accumulated feedback shows the current framing diverges from reality) — constitute the central control mechanisms for keeping a deep-research agent's evolving intermediate representation aligned with reality under pervasive uncertainty.
contributions.quote: We identify three feedback loops as central regulatory mechanisms for an agent's mental model under pervasive uncertainty.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We identify
three feedback loops as central regulatory mecha-
nisms for an agent’s mental model under pervasive
uncertaint

contributions.statement: VeriTrace — a cognitive-graph deep-research framework in which nodes represent concepts under inquiry (each carrying acceptance criteria, accumulated findings, and quality history), edges encode inquiry relations, and three explicit regulatory loops (interpretive update via a graph manager, deviation feedback via a CR-AAP-inspired quality assessor, and schema revision via five structural operations) govern how the graph evolves — improves over the strongest matched baseline by 4.22 pp on DeepResearch Bench Insight and 5.9 pp Overall win rate on DeepConsult using matched Qwen3.5-27B backbones, and achieves the strongest reproducible open-source result on DeepResearch Bench with Config-DeepSeek.
contributions.quote: We present VeriTrace, a cognitive-graph implementation organised around concepts rather than sub-tasks, with evidence preserved under structural revision.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
present VeriTrace, a cognitive-
graph implementation organised around concepts
rather than sub-tasks, with evidence preserved un-
der structural revision.

contributions.statement: Explicit regulatory loops in deep-research agents improve benchmark performance beyond what backbone scale alone can provide; systems that delegate regulation to implicit LLM reasoning show substantial performance drops when the backbone is held fixed, whereas systems with explicit loops do not exhibit the same scale dependency.
contributions.quote: Comprehensive experimental studies show that explicit regulatory loops improve deep research performance beyond backbone scale alone.

contributions.statement: GPT4-generated hypotheses about novel synergistic pairs of FDA-approved non-cancer drugs targeting the MCF7 breast cancer cell line are experimentally validated in the laboratory, with three out of twelve first-round combinations and three out of four second-round combinations achieving synergy scores above positive controls.
contributions.quote: In the first round of laboratory experiments, GPT4 succeeded in discovering three drug combinations (out of 12 tested) with synergy scores above the positive controls. GPT4 then generated new combinations based on its initial results, this generated three more combinations with positive synergy scores (out of four tested).

on its initial results, this generated three more combinations with positive
synergy scores (out of four tested).

contributions.statement: An iterative human–LLM–laboratory workflow in which an LLM proposes drug-combination hypotheses, wet-lab experiments test them, and the LLM refines its proposals based on empirical feedback demonstrably improves the yield of synergistic hits in cancer drug discovery.

contributions.quote: In science, some hallucinations may be useful: novel hypotheses whose validity may be tested by laboratory experiments. Here, we experimentally test the application of LLMs as a source of scientific hypotheses … We conclude that LLMs are a valuable source of scientific hypotheses.

contributions.statement: GALILEO — Generalizable Agentic Laboratory Intelligence for Learning, Experimentation, and Optimization — is a physically embodied AI scientist that couples an Observation-Thought-Action-Summary (OTAS) reasoning loop with robotic peptide synthesis and multimodal phenotyping to close the prediction-to-intervention gap in therapeutic peptide discovery for dynamic membrane systems.
contributions.quote: Here we present Generalizable Agentic Laboratory Intelligence for Learning, Experimentation, and Optimization (GALILEO) as a physically embodied AI scientist for closed-loop therapeutic peptide discovery in dynamic membrane systems. GALILEO is not a de novo peptide generator. Its molecular starting point is a clinically informed peptide prior, and its agentic action is retrieval, local editing, synthesis, quality control, phenotyping, and hypothesis updating under physical feedback.

contributions.statement: CARIBOU — a multi-agent framework that autonomously executes, troubleshoots, and refines bioinformatics workflows within institutional HPC environments using a Singularity/Apptainer-based backend that requires no outbound network connections, making it compatible with real-world cluster security policies.
contributions.quote: we present CARIBOU (Computational AI Research Interface for Bioinformatics, Omics, and Unifying Agents), a multi-agent framework designed for practical deployment within institutional research computing environments… All analyses are executed within reproducible computational environments compatible with Singularity/Apptainer-based high-performance computing (HPC) systems commonly used in academic research

contributions.quote: CARIBOU organizes specialized AI agents through researcher-modifiable blueprints that encode analytical roles, domain knowledge, and workflow guidance… CARIBOU represents agent behavior as researcher-modifiable blueprints, allowing lab-specific judgment and domain standards to be encoded and updated without model fine-tuning or pipeline rewriting

contributions.quote: CARIBOU maintains a single persistent biological analysis state, represented as a living AnnData object, across interactions between multiple specialized AI agents responsible for different analytical tasks

contributions.statement: Empirical evaluation of an autonomous multi-agent bioinformatics system across unit-task benchmarks, a metadata reconstruction challenge spanning six public datasets, and two end-to-end scRNA-seq analyses using Allen Brain Atlas hippocampus and Tabula Sapiens large intestine datasets, with qualitative case studies of adaptive reasoning during live analysis execution.
contributions.quote: We evaluate CARIBOU across unit-task benchmarks, a metadata reconstruction challenge spanning six public datasets, and two end-to-end single-cell RNA-seq analyses using Allen Brain Atlas hippocampus and Tabula Sapiens large intestine datasets, alongside qualitative case studies demonstrating adaptive reasoning during analysis execution

contributions.statement: BiomniBench — a process-level evaluation framework that scores the full agent trajectory against expert-designed, task-specific rubrics across six dimensions (data handling, method selection, statistical rigor, biological interpretation, scientific reasoning, and source reliability) — addresses the dual failures of outcome-only benchmarks: reward hacking on memorized answers and unjust penalization of valid alternative analyses.
contributions.quote: We introduce BiomniBench, a process-level evaluation framework that scores the full agent trajectory against expert-designed, task-specific rubrics applied by an LLM judge, rather than scoring only the final result.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce BiomniBench, a process-level evaluation framework that scores
the full agent trajectory against expert-designed, task-specific rubrics. Our first release,
BiomniBench-DA, contains 100 data-analysis tas

contributions.statement: BiomniBench-DA — 100 biomedical data-analysis tasks spanning 17 task types, 5 disease areas, and a general-biology category, each grounded in a Nature/Cell/Science paper and co-developed with an original author or a 5+ year domain expert — is the first process-level benchmark for LLM agents in real-world biomedical research.
contributions.quote: We therefore release BiomniBench-DA: 100 data analysis tasks curated from papers in Nature, Cell, Science, and similar journals, spanning 17 task types across 5 disease areas and a general-biology category. Each task is co-developed with either an original paper author or a domain expert with 5+ years of research experience.

contributions.statement: The choice of agent harness shifts biomedical data-analysis scores by more than the performance gap between successive frontier model generations, indicating that harness design is a stronger lever than model capability for current LLM agents.
contributions.quote: The agent harness shifts scores by more than the gap between successive model generations.

contributions.statement: Across frontier and open-weight LLMs evaluated on biomedical data analysis, agents reliably ground claims in real sources but consistently underperform on method selection, biological interpretation, and scientific reasoning, identifying these as the primary capability gaps in current systems.
contributions.quote: Dimension-level analysis reveals that agents reliably ground claims in real sources yet consistently fall short on method selection, biological interpretation, and scientific reasoning.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Dimension-level analysis reveals
that agents reliably ground claims in real sources yet consistently fall short on method selection,

contributions.statement: FlowBench — a benchmark that decomposes agentic bioinformatics evaluation into four independently scored layers (planning, fault recovery, biological interpretation, and end-to-end output-fidelity) — exposes capability gaps that single-metric evaluations conflate.
contributions.quote: We introduce FlowBench, a benchmark that decomposes agentic bioinformatics performance into planning, fault recovery, biological interpretation, and end-to-end output-fidelity.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce
FlowBench, a benchmark that decomposes agentic bioinformatics performance into planning, fault
recovery, biological interpretation, and end-to-end output-fidelity.

contributions.statement: FlowAgent — a modular, provider-agnostic agentic bioinformatics framework whose backbone model and scaffold components can be independently swapped or disabled across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini providers on a shared harness — enables controlled attribution of performance to architecture versus model.
contributions.quote: We therefore built FlowAgent, a modular, provider-agnostic framework whose components can be selectively disabled and whose backbone model can be swapped across providers on a shared harness, and used it to evaluate 23 models from three main providers.

contributions.statement: Inferring an appropriate bioinformatics toolchain from biological intent alone is uniformly difficult across all model tiers, compressing 23 evaluated models into a narrow 44–57% pass-rate band, whereas generating a valid plan from a named toolchain is largely solved.
contributions.quote: generating a valid workflow plan from a named toolchain is largely solved, whereas inferring an appropriate toolchain from biological intent alone is uniformly difficult regardless of model tier, compressing all models into a narrow 44–57% pass-rate band.

generating a valid workflow plan from a named

contributions.statement: Dependency-structured workflow planning and a completeness-reflection step are the architectural components that drive plan quality, while adding a same-context validator-driven retry degrades structural correctness rather than improving it.
contributions.quote: ablation shows that the dependency-structured plan and a completeness-reflection step drive performance, while adding a same-context validator-driven retry makes structural quality worse.

ablation shows that the dependency-structured plan and a completeness-reflection
step drive performance, while adding a same-context validator-driven retry makes structural quality

contributions.statement: Fault recovery and data-grounded biological interpretation remain unsolved for current LLM agents: models frequently force clean exits that leave underlying data invalid, and reasoning-tier models are among the least reliable at recognising unrecoverable faults, while data-grounded interpretation consistently lags internal-knowledge recall.
contributions.quote: fault recovery and data-grounded interpretation remain unsolved. Models frequently propose fixes that force a clean exit while leaving the underlying data invalid, and data-grounded interpretation lags internal-knowledge recall by a consistent margin. Safety does not emerge from capability, and reasoning-tier models were among the least reliable at recognising unrecoverable faults.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
fault recovery and data-grounded interpretation remain unsolved. Models frequently
propose fixes that force a clean exit while leaving the underlying data invalid, and data-grounded
interpretation lags internal-knowledge recall by a consistent margin. Safety does not emerge from
capability, and reasoning-tier models were among the least reliable at recognising unrecoverable

contributions.statement: KBase Research Agent — a multi-agent system that combines a LangGraph state-machine for lifecycle orchestration with specialized CrewAI execution crews, grounded in a KBase-specific knowledge graph of the app catalog and retrieval over KBase documentation, to automate genome-assembly and annotation workflows and preserve results as reproducible KBase Narratives.
contributions.quote: We develop an agent framework grounded in KBase-specific knowledge sources, including retrieval over KBase documentation, tutorials and knowledge graph (KG) constructed from the KBase app catalog. … This hybrid architecture combines two agentic frameworks. At the highest level, we leverage LangGraph for flexible, stateful orchestration of the entire research lifecycle-from planning to execution and reporting. For the execution phase, we instantiate specialized, process-driven CrewAI teams.

contributions.quote: We develop an agent system that translates research goals and input data into executable KBase workflows by selecting appropriate KBase applications, configuring their required parameters and inputs, and launching analyses automatically. Because these analyses are executed within KBase, the resulting workflows and outputs are preserved as KBase Narratives.

contributions.quote: The KBase Research Agent provides a complete solution that automates the full scientific workflow, from interactive user goal-setting and metadata collection, through workflow planning and validation, to automated execution and generation of draft manuscripts.

contributions.statement: Autonomous genome analysis of 100 previously uncharacterized bacterial isolates from the JGI IMG/M database — including read QC, assembly, GTDB-Tk taxonomic classification, annotation, reproducible Narratives, and draft manuscripts — produced without human intervention, demonstrating scalable end-to-end scientific workflow automation.
contributions.quote: We further apply the agent to 100 previously unanalyzed bacterial isolate genomes from the JGI IMG/M database, where it autonomously performed read quality control, genome assembly, taxonomic classification with GTDB-Tk, and downstream analysis producing annotated genomes, reproducible Narratives, and draft manuscripts without human intervention.
