contributions.statement: SciHypo is a deep learning framework that generates novel scientific hypotheses by analyzing patterns, relationships, and gaps in scientific literature using GPT-based natural language processing.
contributions.quote: we introduce SciHypo, a deep learning framework leveraging scientific literature to generate fresh hypotheses across diverse fields. SciHypo employs advanced natural language processing, including Generative Pre-trained Transformer to analyze patterns, relationships, and gaps in research.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we introduce SciHypo, a deep learning framework leveraging scientific literature to generate fresh hypotheses across diverse fields. SciHypo employs advanced natural language processing, including Generative Pre-trained Transformer to analyze patterns, relationships, and gaps in research.

contributions.statement: Filter search algorithms improve the relevance and quality of automatically generated scientific hypotheses.
contributions.quote: Filter search algorithms ensure the production of relevant hypotheses.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Filter search algorithms ensure the production of relevant hypotheses.

contributions.statement: Deep learning-based hypothesis generation systems can adapt across diverse scientific disciplines, enabling transdisciplinary research discovery.
contributions.quote: A key strength is SciHypo's adaptability across scientific disciplines, enabling transdisciplinary hypothesis generation.
contributions.quote_verbatim: A key strength is SciHypo's adaptability across scientific disciplines, enabling transdisciplinary hypothesis generation.

contributions.statement: Language models can uncover latent connections in scientific literature to propose novel research trajectories.
contributions.quote: This allows the model to uncover latent connections and propose novel research trajectories.
contributions.quote_verbatim: This allows the model to uncover latent connections and propose novel research trajectories.

contributions.statement: QUASAR — a universal autonomous system that orchestrates complex multiscale atomistic workflows across density functional theory, machine learning potentials, molecular dynamics, and Monte Carlo simulations using LLM-driven reasoning for parameter selection and workflow adaptation.
contributions.quote: we introduce QUASAR, a universal autonomous system for atomistic simulations designed to facilitate production-grade scientific discovery. QUASAR autonomously orchestrates complex multiscale workflows across diverse methods, including density functional theory (DFT), machine learning potentials, molecular dynamics (MD), and Monte Carlo (MC) simulations. The system incorporates robust mechanisms for adaptive planning, context-efficient memory management, and hybrid knowledge retrieval to navigate real-world research scenarios without human intervention.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we introduce QUASAR, a universal autonomous system for atomistic simulations designed to facilitate production-grade scientific discovery. QUASAR autonomously orchestrates complex multiscale workflows across diverse methods, including density functional theory (DFT), machine learning potentials, molecular dynamics (MD), and Monte Carlo (MC) simulations. The system incorporates robust mechanisms for adaptive planning, context-efficient memory management, and hybrid knowledge retrieval to navigate real-world research scenarios without human intervention.

contributions.statement: Double-pass adaptive planning — an initial plan generation followed by a second-pass review that explicitly checks for missing scientific elements before execution.
contributions.quote: we adopted a double-pass planning mechanism: an initial plan produced by the Strategist is subjected to a second-pass review that explicitly checks for missing elements before execution.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we adopted a double-pass planning mechanism: an initial plan produced by the Strategist is subjected to a second-pass review that explicitly checks for missing elements before execution.

contributions.statement: Dynamic tool integration for agentic systems — an extensible architecture that enables flexible integration of new computational tools without requiring major architectural re-engineering or new specialized agent decomposition.
contributions.quote: QUASAR is designed to support dynamic tool integration. Many existing agentic systems rely on hard-coded agent hierarchies and tightly scoped domain-specific tools, which can make their architectures rigid and difficult to extend. Adding new tools to these systems often requires major architectural re-engineering, introducing new specialized agents and functions. QUASAR significantly lowers the barrier to extensibility.
contributions.quote_verbatim: QUASAR is designed to support dynamic tool integration. Many existing agentic systems rely on hard-coded agent hierarchies and tightly scoped domain-specific tools, which can make their architectures rigid and difficult to extend. Adding new tools to these systems often requires major architectural re-engineering, introducing new specialized agents and functions. QUASAR significantly lowers the barrier to extensibility.

contributions.statement: Three-tiered atomistic simulation benchmark — an evaluation framework ranging from routine computational tasks to frontier research challenges, including photocatalyst screening and novel material assessment, for assessing autonomous agentic systems in computational chemistry.
contributions.quote: We benchmark QUASAR against a series of three-tiered tasks, progressing from routine tasks to frontier research challenges, such as photocatalyst screening and novel material assessment.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We benchmark QUASAR against a series of three-tiered tasks, progressing from routine tasks to frontier research challenges, such as photocatalyst screening and novel material assessment.

contributions.statement: Fine-tuning GPT-3 on social psychology literature generates research hypotheses rated by domain experts as comparable to human-generated hypotheses in clarity, originality, and impact.
contributions.quote: In the first approach, we fine-tuned the third-generation generative pre-trained transformer (GPT-3) language model on thousands of abstracts published in more than 50 social psychology journals in the past 55 years as well as on preprint repositories (PsyArXiv). Social psychology experts rated model- and human-generated hypotheses similarly on the dimensions of clarity, originality, and impact.
contributions.quote_verbatim: In the first approach, we fine-tuned the third-generation generative pre-trained transformer (GPT-3) language model on thousands of abstracts published in more than 50 social psychology journals in the past 55 years as well as on preprint repositories (PsyArXiv). Social psychology experts rated model- and human-generated hypotheses similarly on the dimensions of clarity, originality, and impact.

contributions.statement: GPT-4 generates social psychology research hypotheses rated by domain experts as higher in quality than human-generated hypotheses on clarity, originality, impact, plausibility, and relevance.
contributions.quote: In the second approach, without fine-tuning, we generated hypotheses using GPT-4 and found that social psychology experts rated these generated hypotheses as higher in quality than human-generated hypotheses on dimensions of clarity, originality, impact, plausibility, and relevance.
contributions.quote_verbatim: In the second approach, without fine-tuning, we generated hypotheses using GPT-4 and found that social psychology experts rated these generated hypotheses as higher in quality than human-generated hypotheses on dimensions of clarity, originality, impact, plausibility, and relevance.

contributions.statement: ReviewRL, a reinforcement learning framework for generating scientific paper reviews that integrates retrieval-augmented context generation via ArXiv-MCP, supervised fine-tuning, and RL optimization with composite rewards.
contributions.quote: We introduce ReviewRL, a reinforcement learning framework for generating comprehensive and factually grounded scientific paper reviews. Our approach combines: (1) an ArXiv-MCP retrieval-augmented context generation pipeline that incorporates relevant scientific literature, (2) supervised fine-tuning that establishes foundational reviewing capabilities, and (3) a reinforcement learning procedure with a composite reward function that jointly enhances review quality and rating accuracy.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introduce ReviewRL, a reinforcement learning framework for generating comprehensive and factually grounded scientific paper reviews. Our approach combines: (1) an ArXiv-MCP retrieval-augmented context generation pipeline that incorporates relevant scientific literature, (2) supervised fine-tuning that establishes foundational reviewing capabilities, and (3) a reinforcement learning procedure with a composite reward function that jointly enhances review quality and rating accuracy.

contributions.statement: A composite reward function combining rule-based metrics and judge-model-based evaluations for reinforcement learning in scientific review generation, addressing limitations of purely rule-based reward approaches.
contributions.quote: we design a comprehensive reward system incorporating both rule-based metrics and judge-model-based evaluations, effectively mitigating the limitations of purely rule-based methods.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we design a comprehensive reward system incorporating both rule-based metrics and judge-model-based evaluations, effectively mitigating the limitations of purely rule-based methods.

contributions.statement: An action discovery agent method that systematically mines essential tools, databases, and protocols from biomedical literature to construct a unified biomedical action space across 25 domains.
contributions.quote: Biomni first employs an action discovery agent to create the first unified agentic environment – mining essential tools, databases, and protocols from tens of thousands of publications across 25 biomedical domains.

contributions.statement: ResearcherBench — the first benchmark designed to evaluate Deep AI Research Systems (DARS) on frontier scientific questions, assessing systems' ability to provide meaningful insights on unsolved, cutting-edge problems rather than just retrieving and summarizing established knowledge.
contributions.quote: To address this critical gap in evaluation methodology, we introduce ResearcherBench, the first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate DARS capabilities on frontier scientific questions
contributions.quote_verbatim:
To address this critical gap in evaluation methodology, we introduce ResearcherBench, the first benchmark
specifically designed to evaluate DARS capabilities on frontier scientific questions

contributions.statement: A curated dataset of 65 frontier research questions sourced from authentic scientific scenarios including laboratory discussions and interviews with leading AI researchers, spanning 35 distinct AI subjects and categorized into technical details, literature review, and open consulting types.
contributions.quote: We present a carefully curated dataset of 65 high-quality research questions sourced from authentic frontier scenarios, including laboratory discussions, interviews with leading AI researchers, and active scientific forums. These questions span 35 distinct AI research subjects and are categorized into three distinct types.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We present a carefully curated dataset of 65 high-quality research questions sourced
from authentic frontier scenarios, including laboratory discussions, interviews with leading AI researchers,
and active scientific forums. These questions span 35 distinct AI research subjects and are categorized into
three distinct types.

contributions.statement: A dual evaluation framework combining rubric assessment using expert-designed criteria to evaluate insight quality with factual assessment measuring citation accuracy (faithfulness) and coverage (groundedness).
contributions.quote: Our assessment methodology combines rubric assessment with factual assessment. The rubric assessment employs domain expert-crafted evaluation criteria tailored to each specific question, ensuring alignment with human-anchored high-value insights. The factual assessment framework introduces two complementary metrics: faithfulness score and groundedness score, to evaluate the overall factual accuracy and coverage of generated content.

assessment methodology combines rubric assessment with

specific question, ensuring alignment with human-anchored high-value insights. The factual assessment
framework introduces two complementary metrics: faithfulness score and and groundedness score, to evaluate
the overall factual accuracy and coverage of generated content

contributions.statement: OpenAI Deep Research and Gemini Deep Research significantly outperform other DARS systems on frontier questions, with particular strength in open-ended consulting tasks, while exhibiting a paradoxical pattern of high citation accuracy despite limited coverage.
contributions.quote: Results show that OpenAI Deep Research and Gemini Deep Research significantly outperform other systems, with particular strength in open-ended consulting questions... The evaluation also uncovers a paradoxical 'high faithfulness, low groundedness' pattern, and shows that high citation coverage does not necessarily equate to superior insight quality.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
mental limitations. The analysis
reveals their primary strength in exploratory reasoning for open-ended tasks, rather than precise technical or
literature synthesis. The evaluation also uncovers a paradoxical “high faithfulness, low groundedness” pattern,
and shows that high citation coverage does not necessarily equate to superior insight quality.

contributions.statement: El Agente Gráfico — a single-agent framework embedding LLM-driven decision-making in type-safe execution graphs and knowledge-graph persistence — manages context through typed symbolic identifiers for scientific automation.
contributions.quote: In this work, we present El Agente Gráfico, a single-agent framework that embeds LLM-driven decision-making within a type-safe execution environment and dynamic knowledge graphs for external persistence.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
In this work, we presentEl Agente Gráfico, a single-agent framework that embeds
LLM-driven decision-making within a type-safe execution environment and dynamic knowledge graphs
for external persistence.

contributions.statement: Object-graph mapping represents computational state as typed Python objects, enabling persistent storage, consistent tool orchestration, and provenance tracking across heterogeneous scientific workflows.
contributions.quote: Central to our approach is a structured abstraction of scientific concepts and an object-graph mapper that represents computational state as typed Python objects, stored either in memory or persisted in an external knowledge graph. This design enables context management through typed symbolic identifiers rather than raw text, thereby ensuring consistency, supporting provenance tracking, and enabling efficient tool orchestration.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Central to our approach is a structured abstraction of scientific concepts and
an object-graph mapper that represents computational state as typed Python objects, stored either
in memory or persisted in an external knowledge graph. This design enables context management
through typed symbolic identifiers rather than raw text, thereby ensuring consistency, supporting
provenance tracking, and enabling efficient tool orchestration.

contributions.statement: ConceptualAtoms — a unified abstraction layer for molecular and periodic systems — enables zero-copy state transfer and on-the-fly transformations across heterogeneous computational packages.
contributions.quote: Across heterogeneous packages, the ConceptualAtoms class provides a unified in-memory interface for molecular and periodic systems, including charge/multiplicity verification. Package-specific input/output classes compose ConceptualAtoms for state representation, enabling on-the-fly transformations (e.g., conformer sampling followed by electronic-structure calculations) while maintaining direct Python object references for zero-copy state transfer.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
rAcross heterogeneous packages, the ConceptualAtoms class provides a unified in-memory
interface for molecular and periodic systems, including charge/multiplicity verification. Package-specific
input/output classes composeConceptualAtoms for state representation, enabling on-the-fly transformations
(e.g., conformer sampling followed by electronic-structure calculations) while maintaining direct Python object
references for zero-copy state transfer.

contributions.statement: Single LLM-driven agents coupled with type-safe execution engines robustly perform complex, multi-step, and parallel computations in quantum chemistry and materials science applications.
contributions.quote: We evaluate the system by developing an automated benchmarking framework across a suite of university-level quantum chemistry tasks previously evaluated on a multi-agent system, demonstrating that a single agent, when coupled to a reliable execution engine, can robustly perform complex, multi-step, and parallel computations.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We evaluate the system by developing
an automated benchmarking framework across a suite of university-level quantum chemistry tasks
previously evaluated on a multi-agent system, demonstrating that a single agent, when coupled to a
reliable execution engine, can robustly perform complex, multi-step, and parallel computations.

contributions.statement: A spectrum of integration methods for combining experts, LLMs, and data-driven approaches in causal hypothesis generation, ranging from purely knowledge-driven to purely data-driven, with varying degrees of interaction between sources.
contributions.quote: we introduce a spectrum of integration methods for experts, LLMs, and data-driven approaches. We review existing approaches for causal hypothesis generation and classify them within this spectrum.
contributions.quote_verbatim: we introduce a spectrum of integration methods for experts, LLMs, and data-driven approaches. We review existing approaches for causal hypothesis generation and classify them within this spectrum.

contributions.statement: A framework for characterizing LLMs as probabilistic imperfect experts along dimensions of reliability, consistency, uncertainty, and content versus reasoning capabilities.
contributions.quote: Characterizing imperfect experts along dimensions such as (1) reliability, (2) consistency, (3) uncertainty, and (4) content vs. reasoning are emphasized for developing adaptable methods.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Characterizing imperfect experts along dimensions such as (1) reliability, (2) consistency, (3) uncertainty, and (4) content vs. reasoning are emphasized for developing adaptable methods.

contributions.statement: A hybrid causal discovery algorithm combining LLM-based and data-driven approaches for deeper integration in identifying causal structures.
contributions.quote: As an example, our hybrid (LLM + data) causal discovery algorithm illustrates ways for deeper integration.
contributions.quote_verbatim: As an example, our hybrid (LLM + data) causal discovery algorithm illustrates ways for deeper integration.

contributions.statement: CoDaS — A multi-agent framework for biomarker discovery from wearable sensor data, structured as an iterative six-phase process combining hypothesis generation, statistical and machine learning analysis, adversarial validation, mechanistic reasoning, and literature research with human oversight.
contributions.quote: We introduce CoDaS (AI Co-Data-Scientist), a multi-agent system that structures biomarker discovery as an iterative process combining hypothesis generation, statistical analysis, adversarial validation, and literature-grounded reasoning with human oversight using large-scale wearable datasets.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce CoDaS (AICo-Data-Scientist), a multi-agent
system that structures biomarker discovery as an iterative process combining hypothesis generation,
statistical analysis, adversarial validation, and literature-grounded reasoning with human oversight
using large-scale wearable datasets.

contributions.statement: Digital biomarker validation framework operationalizing replication, stability, robustness, and discriminative power via 11 checks including permutation testing, bootstrap confidence intervals, subgroup consistency analysis, and methodological triangulation.
contributions.quote: Each candidate was subjected to a validation battery operationalizing four complementary dimensions: replication, stability, robustness, and discriminative power via 11 checks (including permutation testing, bootstrap confidence intervals, subgroup consistency, and methodological triangulation), consistent with emerging digital biomarker standards.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Each candidate was subjected to a validation battery operationalizing
four complementary dimensions: replication, stability, robustness, and discriminative power via 11
checks (including permutation testing, bootstrap confidence intervals, subgroup consistency, and
methodological triangulation), consistent with emerging digital biomarker standards.

contributions.statement: Sleep variability features (duration and onset variability) are circadian instability-related biomarkers associated with depression severity across independent wearable datasets.
contributions.quote: Across two independent depression cohorts, CoDaS surfaced circadian instability-related features in both datasets, reflected in sleep duration variability (DWB, ρ= 0.252, p < 0.001) and sleep onset variability (GLOBEM, ρ= 0.126, p < 0.001).
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Across two independent depression cohorts, CoDaS surfaced circadian instability-related features
in both datasets, reflected in sleep duration variability (DWB,𝜌= 0.252, 𝑝 < 0.001) and sleep onset
variability (GLOBEM,𝜌= 0.126, 𝑝 < 0.001). I

contributions.statement: A wearable-derived cardiovascular fitness index (steps/resting heart rate ratio) is associated with insulin resistance and metabolic outcomes.
contributions.quote: In a metabolic cohort, CoDaS derived a cardiovascular fitness index (steps/resting heart rate; ρ= -0.374, p < 0.001), and recovered established clinical associations, including the hepatic function ratio (AST/ALT; ρ= -0.375, p < 0.001), a known correlate of insulin resistance.

contributions.statement: Iteris — an explore-plan-execute agentic research loop coordinating multiple research modes (conjecture formulation, numerical experimentation, proof construction, and route review) for open problems in computational mathematics.
contributions.quote: We describe the Iteris system: an explore–plan–execute agentic loop system for distinct research modes in computational mathematics.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We describe the Iteris system: an explore–plan–execute agentic loop system for distinct research modes in computational mathematics.

contributions.statement: A fixed-parameter phase diagram characterizing the asymptotic cost comparison between randomized coordinate descent and conjugate gradient on power-law spectra, identifying regimes where each method dominates in the normalized cost ratio.
contributions.quote: We establish a fixed-parameter phase diagram for the CG-versus-RCD comparison on power-law spectra, giving asymptotic rates for the normalized cost ratio between RCD and CG (Theorem 1).
contributions.quote_verbatim: e establish a fixed-parameter phase diagram for the CG-versus-RCD comparison on power-law spectra, giving asymptotic rates for the normalized cost ratio between RCD and CG (Theorem 1 ).

contributions.statement: A family of low-coherence orthonormal-row matrices demonstrating that QR factorization with column pivoting can fail to select well-conditioned submatrices, refuting a potential positive result.
contributions.quote: We answer the QRCP orthonormal-selection question in the negative by constructing a low-coherence orthonormal-row counterexample family (Theorem 2).
contributions.quote_verbatim: e answer the QRCP orthonormal-selection question in the negative by constructing a low-coherence orthonormal-row counterexample family (Theorem 2 ).

contributions.quote: we introduce Co-Scientist, a multi-agent AI system built on Gemini for structured scientific thinking and hypothesis generation... it formulates demonstrably novel research hypotheses for experimental verification

contributions.statement: DeepReview — a multi-stage structured framework for automated paper review that emulates expert reviewers through novelty assessment, multi-dimensional evaluation criteria, structured analysis, literature retrieval, and reliability verification
contributions.quote: we introduce DeepReview, a multi-stage framework designed to emulate expert reviewers by incorporating structured analysis, literature retrieval, and evidence-based argumentation
contributions.quote_verbatim: we introduce DeepReview, a multi-stage framework designed to emulate expert reviewers by incorporating structured analysis, literature retrieval, and evidence-based argumentation

contributions.statement: DeepReview-13K — a dataset of 13,378 research papers with fine-grained structured intermediate review steps and final assessments capturing expert paper review reasoning processes
contributions.quote: The resulting dataset, DeepReview-13K, consists of raw research papers, structured intermediate review steps, and final assessments.
contributions.quote_verbatim: The resulting dataset, DeepReview-13K , consists of raw research papers, structured intermediate review steps, and final assessments

contributions.statement: DeepReviewer-14B — a 14-billion parameter model trained on structured review reasoning that outperforms larger models like CycleReviewer-70B, achieves 80% win rates against GPT-o1 and DeepSeek-R1, and offers multiple inference modes for balancing efficiency and quality
contributions.quote: we train DeepReviewer-14B, a model that offers three inference modes – Fast, Standard, and Best – allowing users to balance efficiency and response quality
contributions.quote_verbatim: e train DeepReviewer-14B , a model that offers three inference modes – Fast, Standard, and Best – allowing users to balance efficiency and response quality

contributions.statement: DeepReview-Bench — a comprehensive benchmark with 1,286 samples for evaluating paper review systems on both quantitative aspects (rating prediction, ranking, paper selection) and qualitative review generation quality
contributions.quote: We further construct DeepReview-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark containing 1.2K samples, which evaluates both quantitative aspects (rating prediction, quality ranking, and paper selection) and qualitative review generation through LLM-based assessment.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We further construct DeepReview-Bench , a comprehensive benchmark containing 1.2K samples, which evaluates both quantitative aspects (rating prediction, quality ranking, and paper selection) and qualitative review generation through LLM-based assessment

contributions.statement: BioML-bench enables evaluation of AI agents on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks across four domains (protein engineering, single-cell omics, biomedical imaging, drug discovery), where agents must implement complete ML pipelines from task description to validated predictions.
contributions.quote: We introduce BioML-bench, the first benchmarking suite for evaluating AI agents on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks. BioML-bench spans four domains (protein engineering, single-cell omics, biomedical imaging, and drug discovery) with tasks that require agents to parse a task description, build a pipeline, implement models, and submit predictions graded by established metrics (e.g., AUROC, Spearman).
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce BioML-bench, the first benchmarking suite for evaluating AI agents
on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks. BioML-bench spans four domains (protein
engineering, single-cell omics, biomedical imaging, and drug discovery) with
tasks that require agents to parse a task description, build a pipeline, implement
models, and submit predictions graded by established metrics (e.g., AUROC,
Spearman).

contributions.statement: Biomedical-specialized agent architectures do not provide consistent performance advantages over general-purpose ML agents on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks.
contributions.quote: biomedical specialization does not confer a consistent advantage
contributions.quote_verbatim:
biomedical specialization
does not confer a consistent advantage

contributions.statement: Agent success on biomedical ML tasks is more strongly driven by diversity of ML strategies employed (e.g., feature engineering, model stacking) and overall architecture design than by domain-specific biomedical knowledge.
contributions.quote: agents which employed more diverse ML strategies more often tended to score highest, suggesting that architecture and scaffolding may be stronger determinants of performance
contributions.quote_verbatim:
agents which employed

contributions.statement: CompBioBench is a 100-question benchmark spanning genomics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, single-cell analysis, human genetics, and machine learning, evaluating agentic systems on diverse problems requiring multi-step reasoning, tool use, external resource access, and autonomous strategy selection.
contributions.quote: We introduce CompBioBench, a benchmark of 100 diverse tasks for evaluating agentic systems in computational biology.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We introduce CompBioBench, a benchmark of 100 diverse tasks for evaluating agentic systems in
computational biology.

contributions.statement: Using synthetic/augmented data and metadata scrambling/scrubbing enables objective benchmark design for inherently noisy domains, producing unambiguous ground-truth answers while maintaining problem complexity and requiring multi-step reasoning, tool use, and autonomous strategy selection.
contributions.quote: we propose a new benchmark construction strategy based on synthetic/augmented data and metadata scrambling/scrubbing of real datasets to create challenging problems that have a single ground-truth answer and require multi-step reasoning, tool use, bespoke code, and interaction with real-world external resources.

On the hardest questions, Claude Code (Opus 4.6) reaches 69%, Codex CLI (GPT 5.4) reaches 59%, and Gemini CLI (3.1 Pro) reaches 49%.

contributions.statement: SciAgents—a multi-agent framework that integrates large-scale ontological knowledge graphs with specialized LLM-based agents and data retrieval tools, assigning distinct roles through prompt engineering to automate scientific discovery.
contributions.quote: In this work, SciAgents, an approach that leverages three core concepts is presented: (1) large-scale ontological knowledge graphs to organize and interconnect diverse scientific concepts, (2) a suite of large language models (LLMs) and data retrieval tools, and (3) multi-agent systems with in-situ learning capabilities.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
In this work, SciAgents, an approach that
leverages three core concepts is presented: (1) large-scale ontological
knowledge graphs to organize and interconnect diverse scientific concepts,
(2) a suite of large language models (LLMs) and data retrieval tools, and (3)
multi-agent systems with in-situ learning capabilities.

contributions.statement: A novel sub-graph sampling strategy that extracts contextually relevant subgraphs from large ontological knowledge graphs to contextualize and guide multi-agent hypothesis generation and scientific reasoning.
contributions.quote: We implemented a novel sampling strategy to extract relevant sub-graphs from this comprehensive knowledge graph, allowing us to identify and understand the key concepts and their interrelationships.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
We implemented a novel sampling strategy to extract
relevant sub-graphs from this comprehensive knowledge graph,
allowing us to identify and understand the key concepts and their
interrelationships.

contributions.statement: Multi-agent systems with specialized roles can reveal previously hidden interdisciplinary relationships between scientific concepts through knowledge graph reasoning, achieving greater scale, precision, and exploratory power than human research methods.
contributions.quote: Applied to biologically inspired materials, SciAgents reveals hidden interdisciplinary relationships that were previously considered unrelated, achieving a scale, precision, and exploratory power that surpasses human research methods.
contributions.quote_verbatim:
Applied to biologically
inspired materials, SciAgents reveals hidden interdisciplinary relationships
that were previously considered unrelated, achieving a scale, precision, and
exploratory power that surpasses human research methods.

contributions.statement: Large language models can effectively structure scientific knowledge and generate interesting, testable scientific hypotheses, though with elevated error rates.
contributions.quote: generative AI seems to be able to effectively structure vast amounts of scientific knowledge and provide interesting and testable hypotheses
contributions.quote_verbatim: generative AI seems to be able to effectively structure vast amounts of scientific knowledge and provide interesting and testable hypotheses

contributions.statement: A multi-agent scientific discovery framework combining AI-generated hypothesis generation, human curation, automated experimentation, and adversarial peer review to enable synergistic human-AI scientific research.
contributions.quote: The future scientific enterprise may include synergistic efforts with a swarm of "hypothesis machines", challenged by automated experimentation and adversarial peer reviews
contributions.quote_verbatim: The future scientific enterprise may include synergistic efforts with a swarm of “hypothesis machines”, challenged by automated experimentation and adversarial peer reviews

contributions.statement: Peer review reformulated as a multi-turn, long-context dialogue with role-based interactions for authors, reviewers, and decision makers captures the iterative nature of academic peer review.
contributions.quote: we reformulate the peer-review process as a multi-turn, long-context dialogue, incorporating distinct roles for authors, reviewers, and decision makers
contributions.quote_verbatim: we reformulate the peer-review process as a multi-turn, long-context dialogue, incorporating distinct roles for authors, reviewers, and decision makers

contributions.statement: ReviewMT — a dataset of 26,841 papers with 92,017 reviews from ICLR and Nature Communications designed for training and evaluating multi-turn dialogue systems in academic peer review.
contributions.quote: We construct a comprehensive dataset containing over 26,841 papers with 92,017 reviews collected from multiple sources, including the top-tier conference and prestigious journal. This dataset is meticulously designed to facilitate the applications of LLMs for multi-turn dialogues, effectively simulating the complete peer-review process.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We construct a comprehensive dataset containing over 26,841 papers with 92,017 reviews collected from multiple sources, including the top-tier conference and prestigious journal. This dataset is meticulously designed to facilitate the applications of LLMs for multi-turn dialogues, effectively simulating the complete peer-review process.

contributions.statement: Role-specific evaluation metrics for assessing LLM performance in multi-turn peer-review dialogues, measuring response validity, text quality, score accuracy, and decision-making quality for each participant role.
contributions.quote: we propose a series of metrics to evaluate the performance of LLMs for each role under this reformulated peer-review setting, ensuring fair and comprehensive evaluations
contributions.quote_verbatim: we propose a series of metrics to evaluate the performance of LLMs for each role under this reformulated peer-review setting, ensuring fair and comprehensive evaluations

contributions.statement: An interactive web-based system for multimodal peer review simulation integrating textual and visual information through multimodal LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation grounded in web-scale OpenReview data.
contributions.quote: We present the first open-source web demo system that integrates multimodal LLMs to deliver human-aligned review feedback, grounded in publicly available review datasets from OpenReview for the target venue.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We present the first open-source web demo system that integrates multimodal LLMs to deliver human-aligned review feedback, grounded in publicly available review datasets from OpenReview for the target venue.

contributions.statement: Action:Objective[#] — a structured format that distills peer review commentary into traceable, actionable revision items enabling authors to systematically apply feedback for manuscript improvement.
contributions.quote: We propose a structured and actionable to-do format that distills reviewer commentary into traceable revision items, enabling authors to systematically apply feedback for manuscript improvements before submission.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We propose a structured and actionable to-do format that distills reviewer commentary into traceable revision items, enabling authors to systematically apply feedback for manuscript improvements before submission.

contributions.statement: Multimodal perception combining text and visual elements produces more comprehensive and balanced peer review assessments with reduced bias compared to text-only approaches.
contributions.quote: Empirical results demonstrate the critical role of multimodal perception in producing more comprehensive and balanced assessments, reducing bias, and enhancing feedback reliability and usefulness in academic workflows.
contributions.quote_verbatim: Empirical results demonstrate the critical role of multimodal perception in producing more comprehensive and balanced assessments, reducing bias, and enhancing feedback reliability and usefulness in academic workflows.

contributions.statement: Orion — a computer-using AI agent that integrates terminal code execution, graphical interface control, and adaptive multi-step reasoning to automate end-to-end biomedical image analysis and interpretation workflows without requiring bespoke software integrations.
contributions.quote: we present Orion, a computer-using AI agent for biomedical image analysis and interpretation that moves towards lab automation by automating this computational layer of laboratory work. Orion combines large language models with terminal execution, GUI control and adaptive multi-step reasoning in a shared computing environment. It can inspect visual data, operate standard scientific software, mine web resources and conduct end-to-end analysis and interpretation workflows without requiring bespoke software integrations.

contributions.quote: Orion overcomes this via a hierarchical agent architecture that maximized reasoning efficiency while preventing context saturation. In this framework, a terminal-based controller manages global strategy and file organization, while specialized GUI subagents are dynamically spawned for intensive visual tasks or software operation. By "externalizing" intermediate results to the file system, Orion can sustain over 300 sequential actions

contributions.statement: Computer-using agents can learn to operate standard biomedical image analysis software from small numbers of public demonstrations, automatically adapting to new imaging conditions and adjusting analysis parameters through natural-language prompts.
contributions.quote: From a small number of public demos and tutorials, Orion learned to operate widely-used image analysis software, including CellProfiler and QuPath, enabling autonomous quantification of measured cellular and tissue features, characterization of subcellular morphology and segmentation of whole-slide pathology regions. Rather than relying on task-specific tuning, Orion generalized to new imaging settings through natural-language prompts and automatically adjusted analysis parameters to account for variations in cell size and imaging conditions.

contributions.statement: Large language models demonstrate capability in scientific research automation for technical document summarization, literature-informed experimental design, simple statistical analysis tasks, and cross-concept inference without explicit instruction.
contributions.quote: Nevertheless, LLMs did show promise at replicating aspects of the scientific process. For example, the ability to analyze a technical document and extract concise summaries was performed expertly; further, the task of then surveying relevant literature to aid in experimental design, such as finding the marker genes necessary to guide scRNA-seq analyses, was also performed well. Lastly, the system was able to draw inferences and connect related concepts using its background knowledge, without being explicitly told to do so.

contributions.quote: We introduce Idea Novelty Checker, a retrieval-augmented LLM pipeline that automatically evaluates the novelty of scientific ideas. Idea Novelty Checker first collects a broad set of relevant papers using keyword and snippet-based retrieval, then refines this collection through embedding-based filtering followed by facet-based LLM re-ranking.

contributions.statement: Facet-based operationalization of novelty: an idea is novel if it differs in purpose, mechanism, evaluation, or application from papers in prior literature
contributions.quote: An idea is considered novel if it differs from all retrieved papers in at least one core facet for the topic at hand—namely, purpose (i.e., a distinct objective), mechanism (i.e., a distinct technical approach), or evaluation (i.e., a distinct validation method).

contributions.statement: TreeReview—a hierarchical question-answer framework for scientific peer review that recursively decomposes review tasks into sub-questions, answers them using focused paper chunks, and aggregates answers bottom-up with dynamic question expansion for deeper analysis
contributions.quote: we propose TreeReview, a novel framework that models paper review as a hierarchical and bidirectional question-answering process. TreeReview first constructs a tree of review questions by recursively decomposing high-level questions into fine-grained sub-questions and then resolves the question tree by iteratively aggregating answers from leaf to root to get the final review. Crucially, we incorporate a dynamic question expansion mechanism to enable deeper probing by generating follow-up questions when needed.
contributions.quote_verbatim: e propose TreeReview , a novel framework that models paper review as a hierarchical and bidirectional question-answering process. TreeReview first constructs a tree of review questions by recursively decomposing high-level questions into fine-grained sub-questions and then resolves the question tree by iteratively aggregating answers from leaf to root to get the final review. Crucially, we incorporate a dynamic question expansion mechanism to enable deeper probing by generating follow-up questions when needed.

Each problem comprises an encapsulated multi-step analysis with staged data, prompts that define a quantity of interest while otherwise providing minimal guidance, and verifiable answers.

contributions.statement: A GPT-4-based pipeline that generates structured scientific feedback on research papers, providing assessments of significance, novelty, potential acceptance/rejection reasoning, and improvement suggestions.
contributions.quote: Specifically, we developed a Generative Pretrained Transformer 4 (GPT-4)–based scientific feedback generation pipeline that takes the raw PDF of a paper and produces structured feedback... The system is designed to generate constructive feedback across various key aspects, mirroring the review structure of leading interdisciplinary journals and conferences, including significance and novelty, potential reasons for acceptance, potential reasons for rejection, and suggestions for improvement.

contributions.statement: LLM-generated feedback on research papers overlaps with individual human peer reviews at rates comparable to the overlap between two human reviewers, across multiple scientific domains and large-scale datasets.
contributions.quote: The overlap in the points raised by GPT-4 and by human reviewers (average overlap of 30.85% for Nature journals and 39.23% for ICLR) is comparable with the overlap between two human reviewers (average overlap of 28.58% for Nature journals and 35.25% for ICLR).

contributions.statement: Researchers perceive LLM-generated feedback on their own papers as helpful and beneficial, with a majority finding it useful and most rating it competitive with or superior to human peer review feedback.

contributions.statement: Definition of a full research cycle spanning literature understanding, hypothesis formulation, computational implementation, experiment execution with controls, result validation, theoretical interpretation, and scientific writing—operationalized as a holistic evaluation framework for assessing autonomous AI research capabilities beyond isolated benchmarks.
contributions.quote: we adopt a working notion of a full research cycle as encompassing a series of essential capabilities: understanding existing scientific literature with sufficient depth to identify knowledge gaps, formulating testable hypotheses and experimental plans, implementing computational methods through correct code, executing experiments with proper controls and parameter settings, obtaining and validating numerical results, interpreting findings within theoretical frameworks and identifying limitations, and producing clear scientific writing that accurately represents methods and results.

contributions.quote: Deployment proved demanding, requiring substantial debugging and technical expertise, which undermines common claims about the democratization of science with AI. contributions.statement: An open-source framework enabling autonomous agent laboratories to collaboratively develop research by uploading and retrieving findings from a shared preprint server, allowing agents to iteratively build upon each other's discoveries.
contributions.quote: We introduce AgentRxiv —a novel, open-source framework designed to archive and disseminate research outputs from autonomous agents. This platform enables agents to build upon the discoveries of other agents, driving iterative improvements over time.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introduce AgentRxiv —a novel, open-source framework designed to archive and disseminate research outputs from autonomous agents. This platform enables agents to build upon the discoveries of other agents, driving iterative improvements over time.

contributions.statement: Autonomous agents with access to their prior research achieve measurable cumulative performance improvements across research iterations, with accuracy on mathematical reasoning benchmarks improving from 70.2% to 78.2% through newly discovered reasoning techniques.
contributions.quote: We show that each generation of papers produces measurable improvements when agents are given access to AgentRxiv. For instance, accuracy on the MATH-500 benchmark steadily increased from a 70.2% baseline to 78.2% (11.4% relative improvement) using newly discovered techniques like Simultaneous Divergence Averaging (SDA).
contributions.quote_verbatim: We show that each generation of papers produces measurable improvements when agents are given access to AgentRxiv . For instance, accuracy on the MATH-500 benchmark steadily increased from a 70.2% baseline to 78.2% (11.4% relative improvement) using newly discovered techniques like Simultaneous Divergence Averaging (SDA)

contributions.statement: Reasoning strategies discovered by agents on a source benchmark generalize across diverse target benchmarks and multiple language models, with consistent performance improvements averaging 3.3%.
contributions.quote: We demonstrate that reasoning strategies discovered through AgentRxiv on MATH-500 generalize to other benchmarks and language models. Using the highest performing discovered reasoning method by AgentRxiv, our experiments show performance increase across a range of tasks—from GPQA and MMLU-Pro to MedQA—while also revealing consistent gains across five different language models, from DeepSeek-v3 to Gemini-2.0 pro (improving on average by 3.3%).
contributions.quote_verbatim: e demonstrate that reasoning strategies discovered through AgentRxiv on MATH-500 generalize to other benchmarks and language models. Using the highest performing discovered reasoning method by AgentRxiv , our experiments show performance increase across a range of tasks—from GPQA and MMLU-Pro to MedQA—while also revealing consistent gains across five different language models, from DeepSeek-v3 to Gemini-2.0 pro (improving on average by 3.3%).

contributions.statement: Parallelized execution mode for autonomous agent laboratories, allowing multiple agents to run simultaneously while sharing research findings through a centralized platform, accelerates cumulative research progress compared to isolated agent development.
contributions.quote: We introduce a parallelized mode for AgentRxiv, allowing multiple agentic systems to run simultaneously and share findings. We show that this setup accelerates improvements on MATH-500 by +6.0% with 3 parallel labs. We also find that there are a trade-offs between speed and computational efficiency, with discoveries occurring faster but at a higher computational cost.
contributions.quote_verbatim: We introduce a parallelized mode for AgentRxiv , allowing multiple agentic systems to run simultaneously and share findings. We show that this setup accelerates improvements on MATH-500 by +6.0% with 3 parallel labs. We also find that there are a trade-offs between speed and computational efficiency, with discoveries occurring faster but at a higher computational cost

edges.evidence: AI Scientist-v2 eliminates reliance on human-authored templates, adds progressive tree-search methodology and VLM feedback, extending the original fully automated end-to-end pipeline concept
Jr. AI Scientist extends the fully autonomous discovery model to a mentor-guided baseline-extension setting, working iteratively from an existing paper and codebase rather than starting from scratch
PaperQA2 extends PaperQA's modular RAG architecture with enhanced agentic tool use including citation traversal, LLM reranking, and contextual summarization for improved scientific literature tasks
LitQA2 extends the LitQA benchmark to 248 multiple-choice questions with higher difficulty, requiring full-literature retrieval with answers appearing only once or not in abstracts
AI-Researcher orchestrates the complete scientific research pipeline (literature, hypothesis, implementation, validation, manuscript) extending Denario's modular multi-agent research paradigm
Structured world model extends multi-agent coordination to sustain 200+ parallel agent rollouts with 8× improvement in iteration depth, scaling autonomous research far beyond prior systems
MLEvolve extends AlphaEvolve's evolutionary coding agent with hierarchical adaptive code generation, retrospective memory modules, and progressive graph search for improved long-horizon optimization

Robin extends PaperQA's literature search capability by integrating specialized agents (Crow for concise, Falcon for deep reviews) with experimental data analysis agent (Finch) for end-to-end autonomous discovery
Mixture of Agents extends multi-agent coordination by having multiple LLMs independently refine scientific drafts and merge outputs, enabling deeper iterations and disruptive discovery

Framework for collaborative agent laboratories extends multi-agent research by enabling iterative knowledge sharing where agents upload and retrieve findings from shared preprint servers to build upon discoveries
MLEvolve achieves 65.3% average medal rate and outperforms AlphaEvolve on mathematical algorithm optimization tasks, providing empirical validation of the evolutionary coding approach
LLM-agent pipelines with RL-tuned idea generation produce novel and feasible research hypotheses and complete full research cycles, supporting the feasibility of autonomous ML research discovery
Expert-annotated in-context examples improve LLM-based novelty evaluation accuracy by approximately 13%, supporting the four-dimensional evaluation framework's effectiveness

EXP-Bench results quantify specific failure modes (16.1% experimental design misclassification, 39.7% missing implementation components, 29.4% environment misconfiguration) providing empirical support for the identified failure mode taxonomy

Autonomous AI scientist independently reproduces unpublished findings and generates expert-validated novel discoveries across fields, providing evidence that full-process multi-agent systems produce meaningful scientific output
State-of-the-art biomedical QA performance on LAB-Bench DBQA (~54%) and LAB-Bench LitQA (~63%) validates the benchmark's utility for evaluating practical biology research task capabilities

ToolUniverse provides over 600 ML models, datasets, and APIs enabling any language model to be configured as an AI scientist without retraining, supporting the viability and generalization of autonomous discovery frameworks

Benchmark showing strong baseline agents struggle on complete end-to-end scientific discovery cycles provides empirical foundation supporting the characterization of systematic failure modes in autonomous research pipelines

AI-generated research ideas preferentially occupy lower-impact regions of scientific landscape (fewer citations), contradicting findings that autonomous systems reproducibly generate novel, expert-validated discoveries

AI-Researcher orchestrates the same four-stage research pipeline (ideation, proposal, experimentation, publication) that MLR-Agent pioneered, but as a fully autonomous end-to-end system with more comprehensive coverage.
The 80% failure rate in experiment validation and result fabrication provides specific empirical evidence for one of the recurring long-horizon failure modes identified in autonomous research agents.
JudgeEval provides an expert-graded evaluation dataset and framework designed specifically to assess the problem that LLM-judged novelty is poorly correlated with empirical effectiveness.

ToolUniverse extends the single-system approach demonstrated in the Intelligent Agent by providing a general, configurable ecosystem of 600+ tools that enables any LLM to function as an AI scientist without retraining.

ReviewRL extends GPT-4-based peer review generation by adding reinforcement learning optimization with composite rewards, improving upon the baseline approach of using language models directly for review generation.

The finding that LLM-generated feedback overlaps with human reviews at rates comparable to inter-human overlap provides empirical support for the claim that AI-generated feedback can be judged superior to human-written reviews.

Empirical case studies identifying long-horizon failure modes (impatience, context degradation, overconfidence, coordination failure) provide concrete evidence validating the theoretical taxonomy of six failure modes in autonomous research pipelines.

Marco DeepResearch implements the iterative query decomposition technique demonstrated in the query planning work, applying multiple rounds of subquery retrieval to achieve the large-scale deep research capability.

DeepResearch Bench provides a benchmark suite capable of measuring the dual bottlenecks of query planning quality and relevance assessment that were identified as separating proprietary from open-source model performance.

The structured world model with central coordination for parallel agent execution is architecturally incompatible with AutoScientists' decentralized approach that explicitly rejects central orchestration and enables self-organizing teams.
DR Tulu-8B extends Marco DeepResearch's approach by training the first fully open-source deep research model end-to-end with RLER, matching proprietary systems at dramatically lower cost.

The human-machine interaction study refines Chain-of-Ideas by showing that iterative literature extraction and human synthesis yields qualitatively novel research ideas more effectively than the fully automated ideation approach.

Robin extends ExperimentAgent's code translation capability to a complete end-to-end system that includes hypothesis generation and experimental design, creating the first full autonomous discovery workflow with lab-in-the-loop validation.

Empirical validation that structured hypothesis generation via scientific explanations outperforms direct methods provides evidence supporting Co-Scientist's structured approach to generating novel hypotheses conditioned on evidence.
DeepResearch Bench's comprehensive evaluation framework across 100 PhD-level research tasks can assess WebThinker's performance on deep reasoning and scientific report generation.

The corpus of 37,802 AI-generated scientific ideas from four frameworks and six LLMs provides empirical evidence that scientific idea generation capabilities are indeed decoupled from general intelligence, as models with different general performance scores achieve comparable creative results.

SciAgentGym's 1,780 domain-specific scientific tools provide the execution infrastructure and tool-use grounding that enables SciSciGPT's multi-agent research automation across diverse scientific domains.

The 14% to 26% accuracy improvement through iterative test-time scaling with multiple agent trials validates the effectiveness of structured parallel agent coordination for accumulated reasoning progress.

BioML-bench's comprehensive evaluation of end-to-end biomedical ML tasks measures the generalist agent architecture's ability to perform causal gene prioritization, drug repurposing, and other biomedical workflows without task-specific tuning.

The finding that agent success is driven by diversity of ML strategies employed and overall architecture explains the strong generalization observed across heterogeneous biomedical tasks without domain-specific optimization.

The expert-annotated dataset of scientific ideas with novelty judgments provides ground-truth assessment labels for the corpus of 37,802 AI-generated ideas, enabling evaluation of novelty characteristics across generation frameworks.

Contribution 2 demonstrates that LLM-generated research ideas are significantly more novel than human expert ideas, providing empirical support for the idea generation capability that Contribution 0's autonomous discovery system relies upon.

Contribution 6 identifies that 24% of LLM-generated research documents are plagiarized with one-to-one methodological mapping, directly contradicting Contribution 0's claim that the system can autonomously author complete scientific manuscripts.
Contribution 6's finding of pervasive plagiarism in LLM-generated research contradicts Contribution 2's optimistic conclusion that LLM-generated research ideas are of high quality and novelty.

Contribution 9 reports that current coding agents fabricate or fail to validate experimental results in ~80% of attempts, directly contradicting Contribution 0's ability to autonomously design and execute experiments.
Contribution 9 identifies specific failure modes (fabrication and validation failure) that explain the general low success rates in scientific coding tasks reported in Contribution 7.
Contribution 10 demonstrates that covert text injection can almost entirely control LLM-generated peer reviews, undermining the reliability of the LLM-based review systems proposed in Contribution 3.
Contribution 12 identifies inherent flaws in LLM peer review (hallucination and prestige bias), contradicting the viability of the automated review systems in Contribution 3.

Contribution 12's identification of hallucination and prestige bias in LLM peer review contradicts Contribution 5's claim that role-playing frameworks generate specific and contextually grounded reviews.
Contribution 13 generalizes Contribution 0's autonomous discovery concept to a fully distributed multi-agent environment where agents spontaneously self-organize without centralized coordination.

Contribution 15 demonstrates that specializing agents by comment type substantially reduces generic comments and improves review quality, providing a concrete method to strengthen the LLM review systems in Contribution 3.
Contribution 15's finding that specialization improves review quality supports the effectiveness of Contribution 5's role-playing framework by showing that role-specific optimization works.

Contribution 16 extends Contribution 3 by introducing a meta-reviewer agent layer that aggregates multiple LLM-generated reviews into a single consistent decision, building on the multi-agent review approach.
Contribution 16 extends Contribution 5's multi-role framework by adding an aggregation layer that synthesizes individual role-based reviews into coherent decisions.

Contribution 18 shows that general-purpose LLMs are systematically too lenient as reviewers without domain fine-tuning, contradicting Contribution 3's premise that general LLMs can effectively perform peer review.
Contribution 18 demonstrates that role-playing alone is insufficient without domain fine-tuning, contradicting Contribution 5's claim that role-playing frameworks alone improve review quality.

Contribution 20 demonstrates that LLM-generated reviews contain more deficient segments and lack paper-specificity and constructive feedback, undermining Contribution 3's proposal for automated LLM-based peer review systems.

Contribution 21 provides a technique to improve LLM-driven scientific ideation across full researcher profiles, directly supporting the quality of novel ideas that Contribution 2 claims LLMs can generate.

Literature-based and data-driven hypotheses are shown to be empirically complementary, providing theoretical support for the six-agent pipeline's design that combines both approaches across idea generation and hypothesis formulation stages.

The diagnostic framework for AI scientist failure modes (inappropriate benchmark selection, data leakage, metric misuse) provides a structured lens for understanding CORE-Agent's poor performance (21% accuracy) on computational reproducibility tasks despite task-specific optimization.

Graph-based representation of manuscripts that links ideas, claims, evidence, and technical details provides structured organization supporting FactReview's decomposition of manuscripts into review-relevant claims.

Evaluation noise rather than true overfitting explains performance degradation over extended search horizons, helping account for why CORE-Agent achieves only 21% accuracy despite being a task-specific adaptation of AutoGPT.

CRISPR-GPT refines context-aware LLM-driven protocol optimization by specializing it to CRISPR-based gene-editing experiment design across four specific editing modalities with tunable automation levels.

The concrete finding that hypothesis discovery rates collapse to 38.8% at high difficulty provides empirical evidence supporting the broader critique that AI research frameworks fail at robust computational implementation.

OpenLens AI extends context-aware LLM-driven protocol optimization by embedding it as a component within a complete end-to-end pipeline for health informatics that includes literature review, data analysis, and manuscript preparation.

The iterative human-LLM-laboratory workflow extends context-aware protocol optimization by adding empirical feedback loops where wet-lab experiments validate and refine LLM proposals for drug discovery.

The finding that agent harness choice shifts biomedical analysis scores more than model generation differences explains why CORE-Agent achieves only 21% despite being a targeted modification to generalist AutoGPT, suggesting architectural constraints rather than capability limits.

Dependency-structured workflow planning and completeness-reflection steps that improve plan quality provide architectural guidance relevant to optimizing multi-agent research pipelines like the six-agent framework.

The framework characterizing LLMs as probabilistic imperfect experts along dimensions of reliability, consistency, and uncertainty provides theoretical foundation for the empirical finding that LLM-as-judge evaluation has near-zero correlation with PhD-expert ratings.

Automated scientific discovery with robotic laboratory automation extends context-aware protocol optimization by coupling LLM-driven hypothesis design with systematic experimental execution and validation through integrated liquid handling and cell culture systems.

Despite the critique that AI research frameworks require substantial debugging and do not democratize science, the successful iterative human-LLM-laboratory workflow demonstrates their promised value as supervised assistants for hypothesis refinement.
